search_text: Multimodal Multi-objective Optimization: Comparative Study of the State-of-the-Art 多模态多目标优化：最先进技术的比较研究 Multimodal multi-objective problems (MMOPs) commonly arise in real-world problems where distant solutions in decision space correspond to very similar objective values. To obtain all solutions for MMOPs, many multimodal multi-objective evolutionary algorithms (MMEAs) have been proposed. For now, few studies have encompassed most of the recently proposed representative MMEAs and made a comparative comparison. In this study, we first review the related works during the last two decades. Then, we choose 12 state-of-the-art algorithms that utilize different diversity-maintaining techniques and compared their performance on existing test suites. Experimental results indicate the strengths and weaknesses of different techniques on different types of MMOPs, thus providing guidance on how to select/design MMEAs in specific scenarios. 多模态多目标问题 (MMOP) 通常出现在现实世界的问题中，其中决策空间中的遥远解决方案对应于非常相似的目标值。为了获得 MMOP 的所有解，人们提出了许多多模态多目标进化算法（MMEA）。目前，很少有研究涵盖大多数最近提出的代表性 MMEA 并进行比较。在本研究中，我们首先回顾了过去二十年的相关工作。然后，我们选择 12 种最先进的算法，它们利用不同的多样性维护技术，并比较它们在现有测试套件上的性能。实验结果表明了不同技术在不同类型 MMOP 上的优缺点，从而为如何在特定场景中选择/设计 MMEA 提供指导。 本文对现有的多模态多目标进化算法（MMEAs）进行了比较研究，评估其在不同多模态多目标问题（MMOPs）中的性能。 Wenhua Li,Tao Zhang,Rui Wang,Jing Liang cs.NE

authors_text: Flavia D. Frumosu,Nicole N. Lønfeldt,A. -R. Cecilie Mora-Jensen,Sneha Das,Nicklas Leander Lund,A. Katrine Pagsberg,Line K. H. Clemmensen
title: Interpretability by design using computer vision for behavioral sensing in child and adolescent psychiatry
title_zh: 使用计算机视觉进行儿童和青少年精神病学行为感知设计的可解释性
comment: Presented at 2nd Workshop on Interpretable Machine Learning in Healthcare (IMLH) - International Conference on Machine Learning (ICML) 2022
comments: Presented at 2nd Workshop on Interpretable Machine Learning in Healthcare (IMLH) - International Conference on Machine Learning (ICML) 2022
abstract: Observation is an essential tool for understanding and studying human behavior and mental states. However, coding human behavior is a time-consuming, expensive task, in which reliability can be difficult to achieve and bias is a risk. Machine learning (ML) methods offer ways to improve reliability, decrease cost, and scale up behavioral coding for application in clinical and research settings. Here, we use computer vision to derive behavioral codes or concepts of a gold standard behavioral rating system, offering familiar interpretation for mental health professionals. Features were extracted from videos of clinical diagnostic interviews of children and adolescents with and without obsessive-compulsive disorder. Our computationally-derived ratings were comparable to human expert ratings for negative emotions, activity-level/arousal and anxiety. For the attention and positive affect concepts, our ML ratings performed reasonably. However, results for gaze and vocalization indicate a need for improved data quality or additional data modalities.
summary: Observation is an essential tool for understanding and studying human behavior and mental states. However, coding human behavior is a time-consuming, expensive task, in which reliability can be difficult to achieve and bias is a risk. Machine learning (ML) methods offer ways to improve reliability, decrease cost, and scale up behavioral coding for application in clinical and research settings. Here, we use computer vision to derive behavioral codes or concepts of a gold standard behavioral rating system, offering familiar interpretation for mental health professionals. Features were extracted from videos of clinical diagnostic interviews of children and adolescents with and without obsessive-compulsive disorder. Our computationally-derived ratings were comparable to human expert ratings for negative emotions, activity-level/arousal and anxiety. For the attention and positive affect concepts, our ML ratings performed reasonably. However, results for gaze and vocalization indicate a need for improved data quality or additional data modalities.
abstract_zh: Observation is an essential tool for understanding and studying human behavior and mental states. However, coding human behavior is a time-consuming, expensive task, in which reliability can be difficult to achieve and bias is a risk.机器学习 (ML) 方法提供了提高可靠性、降低成本和扩大行为编码在临床和研究环境中应用的方法。在这里，我们使用计算机视觉来推导黄金标准行为评级系统的行为代码或概念，为心理健康专业人员提供熟悉的解释。特征是从患有或不患有强迫症的儿童和青少年的临床诊断访谈视频中提取的。 Our computationally-derived ratings were comparable to human expert ratings for negative emotions, activity-level/arousal and anxiety. For the attention and positive affect concepts, our ML ratings performed reasonably. However, results for gaze and vocalization indicate a need for improved data quality or additional data modalities.

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于计算机视觉的设计方法，用于儿童和青少年精神病学中的行为感知，通过机器学习自动生成可解释的行为评分。
AI.motivation: 人工编码人类行为耗时且易受偏见影响，而机器学习可提升可靠性、降低成本并实现行为编码的规模化应用。
AI.method: 从儿童和青少年的临床诊断访谈视频中提取特征，利用计算机视觉生成与金标准行为评分系统对应的概念，并与人类专家评分进行对比验证。
AI.result: 机器学习评分在负面情绪、活动水平/焦虑方面与人类评分相当，在注意和积极情绪方面表现合理，但眼神和语音数据需改进数据质量或引入多模态数据。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为临床行为编码提供了可解释的自动化工具，但需优化数据质量和多模态融合以提升特定维度的准确性。

authors_text: Nikolaos Zioulis,Georgios Albanis,Petros Drakoulis,Federico Alvarez,Dimitrios Zarpalas,Petros Daras
title: Hybrid Skip: A Biologically Inspired Skip Connection for the UNet Architecture
title_zh: 混合 Skip：UNet 架构的受生物学启发的 Skip 连接
abstract: In this work we introduce a biologically inspired long-range skip connection for the UNet architecture that relies on the perceptual illusion of hybrid images, being images that simultaneously encode two images. The fusion of early encoder features with deeper decoder ones allows UNet models to produce finer-grained dense predictions. While proven in segmentation tasks, the network's benefits are down-weighted for dense regression tasks as these long-range skip connections additionally result in texture transfer artifacts. Specifically for depth estimation, this hurts smoothness and introduces false positive edges which are detrimental to the task due to the depth maps' piece-wise smooth nature. The proposed HybridSkip connections show improved performance in balancing the trade-off between edge preservation, and the minimization of texture transfer artifacts that hurt smoothness. This is achieved by the proper and balanced exchange of information that Hybrid-Skip connections offer between the high and low frequency, encoder and decoder features, respectively.
summary: In this work we introduce a biologically inspired long-range skip connection for the UNet architecture that relies on the perceptual illusion of hybrid images, being images that simultaneously encode two images. The fusion of early encoder features with deeper decoder ones allows UNet models to produce finer-grained dense predictions. While proven in segmentation tasks, the network's benefits are down-weighted for dense regression tasks as these long-range skip connections additionally result in texture transfer artifacts. Specifically for depth estimation, this hurts smoothness and introduces false positive edges which are detrimental to the task due to the depth maps' piece-wise smooth nature. The proposed HybridSkip connections show improved performance in balancing the trade-off between edge preservation, and the minimization of texture transfer artifacts that hurt smoothness. This is achieved by the proper and balanced exchange of information that Hybrid-Skip connections offer between the high and low frequency, encoder and decoder features, respectively.
abstract_zh: 在这项工作中，我们为 UNet 架构引入了一种受生物学启发的远程跳跃连接，该架构依赖于混合图像的感知错觉，即同时编码两个图像的图像。早期编码器特征与更深层次解码器特征的融合使得 UNet 模型能够产生更细粒度的密集预测。虽然在分割任务中得到了证明，但对于密集回归任务，网络的优势会被削弱，因为这些远程跳跃连接还会导致纹理传输伪影。特别是对于深度估计，这会损害平滑度并引入假阳性边缘，由于深度图的分段平滑性质，这对任务有害。所提出的 HybridSkip 连接在平衡边缘保留与损害平滑度的纹理传输伪影最小化之间的权衡方面表现出改进的性能。这是通过 Hybrid-Skip 连接分别在高频和低频、编码器和解码器功能之间提供的适当且平衡的信息交换来实现的。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种受生物学启发的混合跳跃连接，用于改进UNet架构在深度估计等密集回归任务中的表现。
AI.motivation: 传统长距离跳跃连接在分割任务中有效，但会导致深度估计中的纹理转移伪影，破坏深度图的平滑性。
AI.method: 设计HybridSkip连接，通过平衡编码器低频特征与解码器高频特征的信息交换，抑制纹理伪影同时保留边缘细节。
AI.result: 在深度估计任务中，HybridSkip显著降低了虚假边缘和纹理伪影，提升了深度图的平滑性与预测精度。
AI.conclusion: HybridSkip通过生物启发的特征融合机制，有效解决了UNet在密集回归任务中边缘保持与纹理抑制的矛盾。

authors_text: Zhiyuan Cheng,James Liang,Hongjun Choi,Guanhong Tao,Zhiwen Cao,Dongfang Liu,Xiangyu Zhang
title: Physical Attack on Monocular Depth Estimation with Optimal Adversarial Patches
title_zh: 使用最佳对抗性补丁对单目深度估计进行物理攻击
abstract: Deep learning has substantially boosted the performance of Monocular Depth Estimation (MDE), a critical component in fully vision-based autonomous driving (AD) systems (e.g., Tesla and Toyota). In this work, we develop an attack against learning-based MDE. In particular, we use an optimization-based method to systematically generate stealthy physical-object-oriented adversarial patches to attack depth estimation. We balance the stealth and effectiveness of our attack with object-oriented adversarial design, sensitive region localization, and natural style camouflage. Using real-world driving scenarios, we evaluate our attack on concurrent MDE models and a representative downstream task for AD (i.e., 3D object detection). Experimental results show that our method can generate stealthy, effective, and robust adversarial patches for different target objects and models and achieves more than 6 meters mean depth estimation error and 93% attack success rate (ASR) in object detection with a patch of 1/9 of the vehicle's rear area. Field tests on three different driving routes with a real vehicle indicate that we cause over 6 meters mean depth estimation error and reduce the object detection rate from 90.70% to 5.16% in continuous video frames.
summary: Deep learning has substantially boosted the performance of Monocular Depth Estimation (MDE), a critical component in fully vision-based autonomous driving (AD) systems (e.g., Tesla and Toyota). In this work, we develop an attack against learning-based MDE. In particular, we use an optimization-based method to systematically generate stealthy physical-object-oriented adversarial patches to attack depth estimation. We balance the stealth and effectiveness of our attack with object-oriented adversarial design, sensitive region localization, and natural style camouflage. Using real-world driving scenarios, we evaluate our attack on concurrent MDE models and a representative downstream task for AD (i.e., 3D object detection). Experimental results show that our method can generate stealthy, effective, and robust adversarial patches for different target objects and models and achieves more than 6 meters mean depth estimation error and 93% attack success rate (ASR) in object detection with a patch of 1/9 of the vehicle's rear area. Field tests on three different driving routes with a real vehicle indicate that we cause over 6 meters mean depth estimation error and reduce the object detection rate from 90.70% to 5.16% in continuous video frames.
abstract_zh: 深度学习极大地提高了单目深度估计 (MDE) 的性能，这是完全基于视觉的自动驾驶 (AD) 系统（例如特斯拉和丰田）的关键组成部分。在这项工作中，我们开发了一种针对基于学习的 MDE 的攻击。特别是，我们使用基于优化的方法来系统地生成隐形的面向物理对象的对抗性补丁来攻击深度估计。我们通过面向对象的对抗设计、敏感区域定位和自然风格伪装来平衡攻击的隐秘性和有效性。使用真实世界的驾驶场景，我们评估了对并发 MDE 模型的攻击以及 AD 的代表性下游任务（即 3D 对象检测）。实验结果表明，我们的方法可以针对不同的目标对象和模型生成隐秘、有效和鲁棒的对抗性补丁，并在车辆后方区域的 1/9 补丁中实现超过 6 米的平均深度估计误差和 93% 的攻击成功率（ASR）。实车在三种不同行驶路线上的现场测试表明，我们造成了超过 6 米的平均深度估计误差，并将连续视频帧中的目标检测率从 90.70% 降低到 5.16%。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种针对基于深度学习的单目深度估计（MDE）系统的物理攻击方法，通过生成隐蔽的对抗性贴片干扰自动驾驶系统的深度估计和目标检测。
AI.motivation: 单目深度估计是自动驾驶系统的核心技术，但其对物理攻击的鲁棒性不足可能带来安全隐患。本文旨在揭示该技术在现实场景中的潜在漏洞。
AI.method: 采用优化算法生成针对目标物体的隐蔽对抗贴片，结合敏感区域定位和自然风格伪装技术，平衡攻击隐蔽性与效果，并在真实驾驶场景中验证攻击效果。
AI.result: 实验显示，攻击贴片可使深度估计产生6米以上误差，目标检测率从90.70%降至5.16%，且在三种不同路线的实地测试中均保持高攻击成功率（93%）。
AI.conclusion: 本文证明了物理对抗攻击对MDE系统的有效性，揭示了自动驾驶系统在物理安全层面的脆弱性，为相关防御技术研究提供了警示。

authors_text: Jingran Shen,Nikos Tziritas,Georgios Theodoropoulos
title: A Baselined Gated Attention Recurrent Network for Request Prediction in Ridesharing
title_zh: 用于拼车请求预测的基线门控注意力循环网络
comment: 12 pages, 6 figures, accepted by IEEE Access
comments: 12 pages, 6 figures, accepted by IEEE Access
abstract: Ridesharing has received global popularity due to its convenience and cost efficiency for both drivers and passengers and its strong potential to contribute to the implementation of the UN Sustainable Development Goals. As a result, recent years have witnessed an explosion of research interest in the RSODP (Origin-Destination Prediction for Ridesharing) problem with the goal of predicting the future ridesharing requests and providing schedules for vehicles ahead of time. Most of the existing prediction models utilise Deep Learning. However, they fail to effectively consider both spatial and temporal dynamics. In this paper the Baselined Gated Attention Recurrent Network (BGARN), is proposed, which uses graph convolution with multi-head gated attention to extract spatial features, a recurrent module to extract temporal features, and a baselined transferring layer to calculate the final results. The model is implemented with PyTorch and DGL (Deep Graph Library) and is experimentally evaluated using the New York Taxi Demand Dataset. The results show that BGARN outperforms all the other existing models in terms of prediction accuracy.
summary: Ridesharing has received global popularity due to its convenience and cost efficiency for both drivers and passengers and its strong potential to contribute to the implementation of the UN Sustainable Development Goals. As a result, recent years have witnessed an explosion of research interest in the RSODP (Origin-Destination Prediction for Ridesharing) problem with the goal of predicting the future ridesharing requests and providing schedules for vehicles ahead of time. Most of the existing prediction models utilise Deep Learning. However, they fail to effectively consider both spatial and temporal dynamics. In this paper the Baselined Gated Attention Recurrent Network (BGARN), is proposed, which uses graph convolution with multi-head gated attention to extract spatial features, a recurrent module to extract temporal features, and a baselined transferring layer to calculate the final results. The model is implemented with PyTorch and DGL (Deep Graph Library) and is experimentally evaluated using the New York Taxi Demand Dataset. The results show that BGARN outperforms all the other existing models in terms of prediction accuracy.
abstract_zh: 拼车因其为司机和乘客带来的便利和成本效益以及为实现联合国可持续发展目标做出贡献的巨大潜力而​​受到全球欢迎。因此，近年来，人们对 RSODP（拼车起点-目的地预测）问题的研究兴趣激增，其目标是预测未来的拼车请求并提前提供车辆时间表。大多数现有的预测模型都利用深度学习。然而，他们未能有效地考虑空间和时间动态。本文提出了基线门控注意力循环网络（BGARN），它使用带有多头门控注意力的图卷积来提取空间特征，使用循环模块来提取时间特征，并使用基线传输层来计算最终结果。该模型使用 PyTorch 和 DGL（深度图库）实现，并使用纽约出租车需求数据集进行实验评估。结果表明，BGARN 在预测精度方面优于所有其他现有模型。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种结合空间和时间动态的深度学习模型BGARN，用于提升网约车请求预测的准确性。
AI.motivation: 现有深度学习模型未能有效捕捉网约车请求中的空间与时间动态特征，导致预测精度不足。
AI.method: 设计Baselined Gated Attention Recurrent Network (BGARN)，融合图卷积提取空间特征、循环模块提取时间特征，并通过基线迁移层整合结果。
AI.result: 在纽约出租车需求数据集上，BGARN在预测精度上优于所有现有模型。
AI.conclusion: 通过联合建模空间与时间动态特征，BGARN显著提升了网约车请求预测性能。

authors_text: Homer Walke,Jonathan Yang,Albert Yu,Aviral Kumar,Jedrzej Orbik,Avi Singh,Sergey Levine
title: Don't Start From Scratch: Leveraging Prior Data to Automate Robotic Reinforcement Learning
title_zh: 不要从头开始：利用先前的数据来自动化机器人强化学习
abstract: Reinforcement learning (RL) algorithms hold the promise of enabling autonomous skill acquisition for robotic systems. However, in practice, real-world robotic RL typically requires time consuming data collection and frequent human intervention to reset the environment. Moreover, robotic policies learned with RL often fail when deployed beyond the carefully controlled setting in which they were learned. In this work, we study how these challenges can all be tackled by effective utilization of diverse offline datasets collected from previously seen tasks. When faced with a new task, our system adapts previously learned skills to quickly learn to both perform the new task and return the environment to an initial state, effectively performing its own environment reset. Our empirical results demonstrate that incorporating prior data into robotic reinforcement learning enables autonomous learning, substantially improves sample-efficiency of learning, and enables better generalization. Project website: https://sites.google.com/view/ariel-berkeley/
summary: Reinforcement learning (RL) algorithms hold the promise of enabling autonomous skill acquisition for robotic systems. However, in practice, real-world robotic RL typically requires time consuming data collection and frequent human intervention to reset the environment. Moreover, robotic policies learned with RL often fail when deployed beyond the carefully controlled setting in which they were learned. In this work, we study how these challenges can all be tackled by effective utilization of diverse offline datasets collected from previously seen tasks. When faced with a new task, our system adapts previously learned skills to quickly learn to both perform the new task and return the environment to an initial state, effectively performing its own environment reset. Our empirical results demonstrate that incorporating prior data into robotic reinforcement learning enables autonomous learning, substantially improves sample-efficiency of learning, and enables better generalization. Project website: https://sites.google.com/view/ariel-berkeley/
abstract_zh: 强化学习 (RL) 算法有望为机器人系统实现自主技能获取。然而，在实践中，现实世界的机器人强化学习通常需要耗时的数据收集和频繁的人工干预来重置环境。此外，通过强化学习学习的机器人策略在部署到超出严格控制的学习环境时常常会失败。在这项工作中，我们研究如何通过有效利用从以前见过的任务中收集的各种离线数据集来解决这些挑战。当面对新任务时，我们的系统会适应以前学习的技能，以快速学习执行新任务并将环境返回到初始状态，从而有效地执行其自身的环境重置。我们的实证结果表明，将先验数据纳入机器人强化学习可以实现自主学习，大大提高学习的样本效率，并实现更好的泛化。项目网站：https://sites.google.com/view/ariel-berkeley/

AI.tldr: 本文研究如何利用先前任务收集的离线数据集，提升机器人强化学习的自动化水平和泛化能力。
AI.motivation: 现实中的机器人强化学习面临数据收集耗时、依赖人工干预以及策略泛化能力差等挑战，亟需通过有效利用历史数据解决这些问题。
AI.method: 系统通过适应性复用先前任务中学习到的技能，结合环境重置能力，快速完成新任务的学习与环境恢复。
AI.result: 实验表明，引入历史数据可显著提升学习效率、实现自主学习，并增强策略在新环境中的泛化表现。
AI.conclusion: 有效利用离线数据能够突破传统机器人强化学习的局限，为实际应用提供更高效、鲁棒的解决方案。

ai_content.tldr: 本文研究如何利用先前任务收集的离线数据集，提升机器人强化学习的自动化水平和泛化能力。
ai_content.motivation: 现实中的机器人强化学习面临数据收集耗时、依赖人工干预以及策略泛化能力差等挑战，亟需通过有效利用历史数据解决这些问题。
ai_content.method: 系统通过适应性复用先前任务中学习到的技能，结合环境重置能力，快速完成新任务的学习与环境恢复。
ai_content.result: 实验表明，引入历史数据可显著提升学习效率、实现自主学习，并增强策略在新环境中的泛化表现。
ai_content.conclusion: 有效利用离线数据能够突破传统机器人强化学习的局限，为实际应用提供更高效、鲁棒的解决方案。

search_text: Don't Start From Scratch: Leveraging Prior Data to Automate Robotic Reinforcement Learning 不要从头开始：利用先前的数据来自动化机器人强化学习 Reinforcement learning (RL) algorithms hold the promise of enabling autonomous skill acquisition for robotic systems. However, in practice, real-world robotic RL typically requires time consuming data collection and frequent human intervention to reset the environment. Moreover, robotic policies learned with RL often fail when deployed beyond the carefully controlled setting in which they were learned. In this work, we study how these challenges can all be tackled by effective utilization of diverse offline datasets collected from previously seen tasks. When faced with a new task, our system adapts previously learned skills to quickly learn to both perform the new task and return the environment to an initial state, effectively performing its own environment reset. Our empirical results demonstrate that incorporating prior data into robotic reinforcement learning enables autonomous learning, substantially improves sample-efficiency of learning, and enables better generalization. Project website: https://sites.google.com/view/ariel-berkeley/ 强化学习 (RL) 算法有望为机器人系统实现自主技能获取。然而，在实践中，现实世界的机器人强化学习通常需要耗时的数据收集和频繁的人工干预来重置环境。此外，通过强化学习学习的机器人策略在部署到超出严格控制的学习环境时常常会失败。在这项工作中，我们研究如何通过有效利用从以前见过的任务中收集的各种离线数据集来解决这些挑战。当面对新任务时，我们的系统会适应以前学习的技能，以快速学习执行新任务并将环境返回到初始状态，从而有效地执行其自身的环境重置。我们的实证结果表明，将先验数据纳入机器人强化学习可以实现自主学习，大大提高学习的样本效率，并实现更好的泛化。项目网站：https://sites.google.com/view/ariel-berkeley/ 本文研究如何利用先前任务收集的离线数据集，提升机器人强化学习的自动化水平和泛化能力。 Homer Walke,Jonathan Yang,Albert Yu,Aviral Kumar,Jedrzej Orbik,Avi Singh,Sergey Levine cs.RO,cs.LG

authors_text: Zihan Zhao,Yanfeng Wang,Yu Wang
title: Multi-level Fusion of Wav2vec 2.0 and BERT for Multimodal Emotion Recognition
title_zh: Wav2vec 2.0 和 BERT 的多级融合用于多模态情感识别
abstract: The research and applications of multimodal emotion recognition have become increasingly popular recently. However, multimodal emotion recognition faces the challenge of lack of data. To solve this problem, we propose to use transfer learning which leverages state-of-the-art pre-trained models including wav2vec 2.0 and BERT for this task. Multi-level fusion approaches including coattention-based early fusion and late fusion with the models trained on both embeddings are explored. Also, a multi-granularity framework which extracts not only frame-level speech embeddings but also segment-level embeddings including phone, syllable and word-level speech embeddings is proposed to further boost the performance. By combining our coattention-based early fusion model and late fusion model with the multi-granularity feature extraction framework, we obtain result that outperforms best baseline approaches by 1.3% unweighted accuracy (UA) on the IEMOCAP dataset.
summary: The research and applications of multimodal emotion recognition have become increasingly popular recently. However, multimodal emotion recognition faces the challenge of lack of data. To solve this problem, we propose to use transfer learning which leverages state-of-the-art pre-trained models including wav2vec 2.0 and BERT for this task. Multi-level fusion approaches including coattention-based early fusion and late fusion with the models trained on both embeddings are explored. Also, a multi-granularity framework which extracts not only frame-level speech embeddings but also segment-level embeddings including phone, syllable and word-level speech embeddings is proposed to further boost the performance. By combining our coattention-based early fusion model and late fusion model with the multi-granularity feature extraction framework, we obtain result that outperforms best baseline approaches by 1.3% unweighted accuracy (UA) on the IEMOCAP dataset.
abstract_zh: 近年来，多模态情感识别的研究和应用日益热门。然而，多模态情感识别面临数据缺乏的挑战。为了解决这个问题，我们建议使用迁移学习，该学习利用最先进的预训练模型（包括 wav2vec 2.0 和 BERT）来完成此任务。探索了多级融合方法，包括基于共同注意的早期融合和后期融合以及在两种嵌入上训练的模型。此外，还提出了一种多粒度框架，不仅提取帧级语音嵌入，还提取分段级嵌入，包括音素、音节和单词级语音嵌入，以进一步提高性能。通过将基于共同注意力的早期融合模型和后期融合模型与多粒度特征提取框架相结合，我们在 IEMOCAP 数据集上获得了比最佳基线方法高出 1.3% 的未加权准确度 (UA) 的结果。

AI.tldr: 提出结合Wav2vec 2.0和BERT的多级融合方法，用于提升多模态情绪识别性能
AI.motivation: 多模态情绪识别因数据不足面临挑战，需利用预训练模型迁移学习提升性能
AI.method: 采用共注意力机制的早期融合与晚期融合，结合多粒度语音特征提取框架，整合帧级与语义级嵌入
AI.result: 在IEMOCAP数据集上，相比最佳基线方法，无权准确率（UA）提升1.3%
AI.conclusion: 多级融合与多粒度特征提取有效提升多模态情绪识别性能，验证了迁移学习的潜力

However, multimodal emotion recognition faces the challenge of lack of data. authors_text: Yixiong Liang,Shuo Feng,Qing Liu,Hulin Kuang,Jianfeng Liu,Liyan Liao,Yun Du,Jianxin Wang
title: Exploring Contextual Relationships for Cervical Abnormal Cell Detection
title_zh: 探索宫颈异常细胞检测的上下文关系
abstract: Cervical abnormal cell detection is a challenging task as the morphological discrepancies between abnormal and normal cells are usually subtle. To determine whether a cervical cell is normal or abnormal, cytopathologists always take surrounding cells as references to identify its abnormality. To mimic these behaviors, we propose to explore contextual relationships to boost the performance of cervical abnormal cell detection. Specifically, both contextual relationships between cells and cell-to-global images are exploited to enhance features of each region of interest (RoI) proposals. Accordingly, two modules, dubbed as RoI-relationship attention module (RRAM) and global RoI attention module (GRAM), are developed and their combination strategies are also investigated. We establish a strong baseline by using Double-Head Faster R-CNN with feature pyramid network (FPN) and integrate our RRAM and GRAM into it to validate the effectiveness of the proposed modules. Experiments conducted on a large cervical cell detection dataset reveal that the introduction of RRAM and GRAM both achieves better average precision (AP) than the baseline methods. Moreover, when cascading RRAM and GRAM, our method outperforms the state-of-the-art (SOTA) methods. Furthermore, we also show the proposed feature enhancing scheme can facilitate both image-level and smear-level classification. The code and trained models are publicly available at https://github.com/CVIU-CSU/CR4CACD.
summary: Cervical abnormal cell detection is a challenging task as the morphological discrepancies between abnormal and normal cells are usually subtle. To determine whether a cervical cell is normal or abnormal, cytopathologists always take surrounding cells as references to identify its abnormality. To mimic these behaviors, we propose to explore contextual relationships to boost the performance of cervical abnormal cell detection. Specifically, both contextual relationships between cells and cell-to-global images are exploited to enhance features of each region of interest (RoI) proposals. Accordingly, two modules, dubbed as RoI-relationship attention module (RRAM) and global RoI attention module (GRAM), are developed and their combination strategies are also investigated. We establish a strong baseline by using Double-Head Faster R-CNN with feature pyramid network (FPN) and integrate our RRAM and GRAM into it to validate the effectiveness of the proposed modules. Experiments conducted on a large cervical cell detection dataset reveal that the introduction of RRAM and GRAM both achieves better average precision (AP) than the baseline methods. Moreover, when cascading RRAM and GRAM, our method outperforms the state-of-the-art (SOTA) methods. Furthermore, we also show the proposed feature enhancing scheme can facilitate both image-level and smear-level classification. The code and trained models are publicly available at https://github.com/CVIU-CSU/CR4CACD.
abstract_zh: 宫颈异常细胞检测是一项具有挑战性的任务，因为异常细胞和正常细胞之间的形态差异通常很微妙。为了确定宫颈细胞是否正常或异常，细胞病理学家总是以周围细胞作为参考来识别其异常。为了模仿这些行为，我们建议探索上下文关系以提高宫颈异常细胞检测的性能。具体来说，利用细胞之间的上下文关系和细胞到全局图像来增强每个感兴趣区域（RoI）提案的特征。因此，开发了两个模块，称为RoI关系注意模块（RRAM）和全局RoI注意模块（GRAM），并研究了它们的组合策略。我们通过使用带有特征金字塔网络（FPN）的双头 Faster R-CNN 建立了强大的基线，并将我们的 RRAM 和 GRAM 集成到其中以验证所提出模块的有效性。在大型宫颈细胞检测数据集上进行的实验表明，RRAM 和 GRAM 的引入均比基线方法获得了更好的平均精度（AP）。此外，当级联 RRAM 和 GRAM 时，我们的方法优于最先进的 (SOTA) 方法。此外，我们还表明所提出的特征增强方案可以促进图像级和涂抹级分类。代码和训练模型可在 https://github.com/CVIU-CSU/CR4CACD 上公开获取。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在通过探索细胞间的上下文关系，提升宫颈异常细胞检测的性能。
AI.motivation: 由于正常与异常细胞的形态差异细微，病理学家通常参考周围细胞进行判断，因此需模仿人类行为以增强检测效果。
AI.method: 提出两种模块（RRAM和GRAM），分别利用细胞间关系及全局图像信息增强ROI特征，并集成到双头Faster R-CNN框架中。
AI.result: 实验表明，RRAM和GRAM均优于基线方法，级联使用时超越现有最优方法，且特征增强方案提升了图像级和涂片级分类效果。
AI.conclusion: 上下文关系建模有效提升了检测性能，证明局部与全局信息结合的重要性，代码已开源。

authors_text: Owen Millwood,Jack Miskelly,Bohao Yang,Prosanta Gope,Elif Kavun,Chenghua Lin
title: PUF-Phenotype: A Robust and Noise-Resilient Approach to Aid Intra-Group-based Authentication with DRAM-PUFs Using Machine Learning
title_zh: PUF-Phenotype：一种稳健且抗噪声的方法，可利用机器学习帮助 DRAM-PUF 进行基于组内的身份验证
comment: 13 pages main text, 7 pages supplementary material (total 20 pages), 8 figures, submitted to IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
comments: 13 pages main text, 7 pages supplementary material (total 20 pages), 8 figures, submitted to IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: As the demand for highly secure and dependable lightweight systems increases in the modern world, Physically Unclonable Functions (PUFs) continue to promise a lightweight alternative to high-cost encryption techniques and secure key storage. While the security features promised by PUFs are highly attractive for secure system designers, they have been shown to be vulnerable to various sophisticated attacks - most notably Machine Learning (ML) based modelling attacks (ML-MA) which attempt to digitally clone the PUF behaviour and thus undermine their security. More recent ML-MA have even exploited publicly known helper data required for PUF error correction in order to predict PUF responses without requiring knowledge of response data. In response to this, research is beginning to emerge regarding the authentication of PUF devices with the assistance of ML as opposed to traditional PUF techniques of storage and comparison of pre-known Challenge-Response pairs (CRPs). In this article, we propose a classification system using ML based on a novel `PUF-Phenotype' concept to accurately identify the origin and determine the validity of noisy memory derived (DRAM) PUF responses as an alternative to helper data-reliant denoising techniques. To our best knowledge, we are the first to perform classification over multiple devices per model to enable a group-based PUF authentication scheme. We achieve up to 98\% classification accuracy using a modified deep convolutional neural network (CNN) for feature extraction in conjunction with several well-established classifiers. We also experimentally verified the performance of our model on a Raspberry Pi device to determine the suitability of deploying our proposed model in a resource-constrained environment.
summary: As the demand for highly secure and dependable lightweight systems increases in the modern world, Physically Unclonable Functions (PUFs) continue to promise a lightweight alternative to high-cost encryption techniques and secure key storage. While the security features promised by PUFs are highly attractive for secure system designers, they have been shown to be vulnerable to various sophisticated attacks - most notably Machine Learning (ML) based modelling attacks (ML-MA) which attempt to digitally clone the PUF behaviour and thus undermine their security. More recent ML-MA have even exploited publicly known helper data required for PUF error correction in order to predict PUF responses without requiring knowledge of response data. In response to this, research is beginning to emerge regarding the authentication of PUF devices with the assistance of ML as opposed to traditional PUF techniques of storage and comparison of pre-known Challenge-Response pairs (CRPs). In this article, we propose a classification system using ML based on a novel `PUF-Phenotype' concept to accurately identify the origin and determine the validity of noisy memory derived (DRAM) PUF responses as an alternative to helper data-reliant denoising techniques. To our best knowledge, we are the first to perform classification over multiple devices per model to enable a group-based PUF authentication scheme. We achieve up to 98\% classification accuracy using a modified deep convolutional neural network (CNN) for feature extraction in conjunction with several well-established classifiers. We also experimentally verified the performance of our model on a Raspberry Pi device to determine the suitability of deploying our proposed model in a resource-constrained environment.
abstract_zh: 随着现代世界对高度安全、可靠的轻量级系统的需求不断增加，物理不可克隆功能 (PUF) 继续有望成为高成本加密技术和安全密钥存储的轻量级替代方案。虽然 PUF 承诺的安全功能对于安全系统设计人员来说非常有吸引力，但它们已被证明容易受到各种复杂的攻击 - 最明显的是基于机器学习 (ML) 的建模攻击 (ML-MA)，这些攻击试图以数字方式克隆 PUF 行为，从而破坏其安全性。最近的 ML-MA 甚至利用 PUF 纠错所需的公知辅助数据来预测 PUF 响应，而无需了解响应数据。针对这一点，关于在 ML 的帮助下对 PUF 设备进行身份验证的研究开始出现，而不是传统的 PUF 存储技术和预先已知的质询响应对 (CRP) 的比较。在本文中，我们提出了一种基于新颖的“PUF-表型”概念的机器学习分类系统，以准确识别起源并确定噪声内存派生 (DRAM) PUF 响应的有效性，作为辅助数据依赖型去噪技术的替代方案。据我们所知，我们是第一个对每个模型的多个设备进行分类以启用基于组的 PUF 身份验证方案的公司。我们使用改进的深度卷积神经网络 (CNN) 结合几个成熟的分类器进行特征提取，实现了高达 98% 的分类准确率。我们还通过实验验证了我们的模型在 Raspberry Pi 设备上的性能，以确定在资源受限的环境中部署我们提出的模型的适用性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出基于机器学习的PUF分类系统，通过DRAM-PUF的噪声内存响应特征实现组内认证，替代传统helper数据去噪技术。
AI.motivation: 传统PUF认证方法易受机器学习建模攻击，且依赖helper数据的去噪技术存在安全隐患，需探索更安全的组内认证方案。
AI.method: 提出PUF-Phenotype概念，利用修改后的深度卷积神经网络提取特征并结合多分类器，实现跨设备的组内PUF响应分类。
AI.result: 在树莓派设备上验证模型，分类准确率达98%，证明了方法在资源受限环境中的可行性与高效性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过机器学习分类替代传统helper数据技术，有效提升DRAM-PUF认证的抗噪声能力和安全性，为轻量级系统提供新思路。

authors_text: Alan J. X. Guo,Cong Liang,Qing-Hu Hou
title: Deep Squared Euclidean Approximation to the Levenshtein Distance for DNA Storage
title_zh: DNA 存储编辑距离的深平方欧几里得近似
abstract: Storing information in DNA molecules is of great interest because of its advantages in longevity, high storage density, and low maintenance cost. A key step in the DNA storage pipeline is to efficiently cluster the retrieved DNA sequences according to their similarities. Levenshtein distance is the most suitable metric on the similarity between two DNA sequences, but it is inferior in terms of computational complexity and less compatible with mature clustering algorithms. In this work, we propose a novel deep squared Euclidean embedding for DNA sequences using Siamese neural network, squared Euclidean embedding, and chi-squared regression. The Levenshtein distance is approximated by the squared Euclidean distance between the embedding vectors, which is fast calculated and clustering algorithm friendly. The proposed approach is analyzed theoretically and experimentally. The results show that the proposed embedding is efficient and robust.
summary: Storing information in DNA molecules is of great interest because of its advantages in longevity, high storage density, and low maintenance cost. A key step in the DNA storage pipeline is to efficiently cluster the retrieved DNA sequences according to their similarities. Levenshtein distance is the most suitable metric on the similarity between two DNA sequences, but it is inferior in terms of computational complexity and less compatible with mature clustering algorithms. In this work, we propose a novel deep squared Euclidean embedding for DNA sequences using Siamese neural network, squared Euclidean embedding, and chi-squared regression. The Levenshtein distance is approximated by the squared Euclidean distance between the embedding vectors, which is fast calculated and clustering algorithm friendly. The proposed approach is analyzed theoretically and experimentally. The results show that the proposed embedding is efficient and robust.
abstract_zh: 在DNA分子中存储信息因其寿命长、存储密度高和维护成本低等优点而引起人们极大的兴趣。 DNA 存储管道中的一个关键步骤是根据检索到的 DNA 序列的相似性对其进行有效聚类。编辑距离是衡量两个 DNA 序列之间相似性的最合适的度量标准，但它在计算复杂性方面较差，并且与成熟的聚类算法不太兼容。在这项工作中，我们使用孪生神经网络、平方欧几里德嵌入和卡方回归提出了一种新颖的 DNA 序列深度平方欧几里德嵌入。编辑距离近似于嵌入向量之间的欧氏距离平方，计算速度快且聚类算法友好。对所提出的方法进行了理论和实验分析。结果表明，所提出的嵌入是有效且稳健的。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于深度平方欧几里得嵌入的近似方法，用于DNA存储中DNA序列的相似性度量。
AI.motivation: DNA存储需要高效聚类DNA序列，但莱文斯坦距离计算复杂且不兼容现有聚类算法，需寻找更高效的替代方案。
AI.method: 采用孪生神经网络、平方欧几里得嵌入和卡方回归，将莱文斯坦距离近似为嵌入向量间的平方欧氏距离。
AI.result: 实验和理论分析表明，该嵌入方法在计算效率和鲁棒性方面表现优异。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为DNA序列聚类提供了高效且兼容的相似性度量解决方案，解决了传统莱文斯坦距离的局限性。

authors_text: Sen Zhang,Jing Zhang,Dacheng Tao
title: Towards Scale-Aware, Robust, and Generalizable Unsupervised Monocular Depth Estimation by Integrating IMU Motion Dynamics
title_zh: 通过集成 IMU 运动动力学实现尺度感知、鲁棒且可推广的无监督单目深度估计
comment: Accepted to ECCV 2022. Code is released at https://github.com/SenZHANG-GitHub/ekf-imu-depth
comments: Accepted to ECCV 2022. Code is released at https://github.com/SenZHANG-GitHub/ekf-imu-depth
abstract: Unsupervised monocular depth and ego-motion estimation has drawn extensive research attention in recent years. Although current methods have reached a high up-to-scale accuracy, they usually fail to learn the true scale metric due to the inherent scale ambiguity from training with monocular sequences. In this work, we tackle this problem and propose DynaDepth, a novel scale-aware framework that integrates information from vision and IMU motion dynamics. Specifically, we first propose an IMU photometric loss and a cross-sensor photometric consistency loss to provide dense supervision and absolute scales. To fully exploit the complementary information from both sensors, we further drive a differentiable camera-centric extended Kalman filter (EKF) to update the IMU preintegrated motions when observing visual measurements. In addition, the EKF formulation enables learning an ego-motion uncertainty measure, which is non-trivial for unsupervised methods. By leveraging IMU during training, DynaDepth not only learns an absolute scale, but also provides a better generalization ability and robustness against vision degradation such as illumination change and moving objects. We validate the effectiveness of DynaDepth by conducting extensive experiments and simulations on the KITTI and Make3D datasets.
summary: Unsupervised monocular depth and ego-motion estimation has drawn extensive research attention in recent years. Although current methods have reached a high up-to-scale accuracy, they usually fail to learn the true scale metric due to the inherent scale ambiguity from training with monocular sequences. In this work, we tackle this problem and propose DynaDepth, a novel scale-aware framework that integrates information from vision and IMU motion dynamics. Specifically, we first propose an IMU photometric loss and a cross-sensor photometric consistency loss to provide dense supervision and absolute scales. To fully exploit the complementary information from both sensors, we further drive a differentiable camera-centric extended Kalman filter (EKF) to update the IMU preintegrated motions when observing visual measurements. In addition, the EKF formulation enables learning an ego-motion uncertainty measure, which is non-trivial for unsupervised methods. By leveraging IMU during training, DynaDepth not only learns an absolute scale, but also provides a better generalization ability and robustness against vision degradation such as illumination change and moving objects. We validate the effectiveness of DynaDepth by conducting extensive experiments and simulations on the KITTI and Make3D datasets.
abstract_zh: 近年来，无监督单眼深度和自我运动估计引起了广泛的研究关注。尽管当前的方法已经达到了较高的尺度精度，但由于单目序列训练固有的尺度模糊性，它们通常无法学习真实的尺度度量。在这项工作中，我们解决了这个问题并提出了 DynaDepth，这是一种新颖的尺度感知框架，集成了来自视觉和 IMU 运动动力学的信息。具体来说，我们首先提出 IMU 光度损失和跨传感器光度一致性损失，以提供密集的监督和绝对尺度。为了充分利用两个传感器的互补信息，我们进一步驱动以相机为中心的可微分扩展卡尔曼滤波器（EKF），以在观察视觉测量时更新 IMU 预积分运动。此外，EKF 公式能够学习自我运动不确定性度量，这对于无监督方法来说并非易事。通过在训练过程中利用 IMU，DynaDepth 不仅可以学习绝对尺度，还可以针对光照变化和移动物体等视觉退化提供更好的泛化能力和鲁棒性。我们通过在 KITTI 和 Make3D 数据集上进行广泛的实验和模拟来验证 DynaDepth 的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出DynaDepth框架，通过整合IMU运动动态信息，解决单目深度估计中的尺度模糊问题，并提升模型鲁棒性和泛化能力。
AI.motivation: 现有单目深度估计方法因尺度模糊无法学习真实尺度，且在光照变化和动态物体等场景下表现不稳定，需引入IMU信息以提升性能。
AI.method: 设计IMU光度损失和跨传感器一致性损失提供密集监督；结合可微分相机中心扩展卡尔曼滤波器（EKF）融合IMU预积分运动与视觉观测，学习自运动不确定性。
AI.result: 在KITTI和Make3D数据集上验证，DynaDepth在尺度准确性、光照变化和动态物体场景下的鲁棒性及跨场景泛化能力显著优于现有方法。
AI.conclusion: 通过IMU与视觉的深度融合及EKF建模，DynaDepth实现了尺度感知、鲁棒且通用的单目深度估计，为无监督学习提供了新思路。

search_text: Towards Scale-Aware, Robust, and Generalizable Unsupervised Monocular Depth Estimation by Integrating IMU Motion Dynamics 通过集成 IMU 运动动力学实现尺度感知、鲁棒且可推广的无监督单目深度估计 Unsupervised monocular depth and ego-motion estimation has drawn extensive research attention in recent years. Although current methods have reached a high up-to-scale accuracy, they usually fail to learn the true scale metric due to the inherent scale ambiguity from training with monocular sequences. In this work, we tackle this problem and propose DynaDepth, a novel scale-aware framework that integrates information from vision and IMU motion dynamics. Specifically, we first propose an IMU photometric loss and a cross-sensor photometric consistency loss to provide dense supervision and absolute scales. To fully exploit the complementary information from both sensors, we further drive a differentiable camera-centric extended Kalman filter (EKF) to update the IMU preintegrated motions when observing visual measurements. In addition, the EKF formulation enables learning an ego-motion uncertainty measure, which is non-trivial for unsupervised methods. By leveraging IMU during training, DynaDepth not only learns an absolute scale, but also provides a better generalization ability and robustness against vision degradation such as illumination change and moving objects. We validate the effectiveness of DynaDepth by conducting extensive experiments and simulations on the KITTI and Make3D datasets. 近年来，无监督单眼深度和自我运动估计引起了广泛的研究关注。尽管当前的方法已经达到了较高的尺度精度，但由于单目序列训练固有的尺度模糊性，它们通常无法学习真实的尺度度量。在这项工作中，我们解决了这个问题并提出了 DynaDepth，这是一种新颖的尺度感知框架，集成了来自视觉和 IMU 运动动力学的信息。具体来说，我们首先提出 IMU 光度损失和跨传感器光度一致性损失，以提供密集的监督和绝对尺度。为了充分利用两个传感器的互补信息，我们进一步驱动以相机为中心的可微分扩展卡尔曼滤波器（EKF），以在观察视觉测量时更新 IMU 预积分运动。此外，EKF 公式能够学习自我运动不确定性度量，这对于无监督方法来说并非易事。通过在训练过程中利用 IMU，DynaDepth 不仅可以学习绝对尺度，还可以针对光照变化和移动物体等视觉退化提供更好的泛化能力和鲁棒性。我们通过在 KITTI 和 Make3D 数据集上进行广泛的实验和模拟来验证 DynaDepth 的有效性。 本文提出DynaDepth框架，通过整合IMU运动动态信息，解决单目深度估计中的尺度模糊问题，并提升模型鲁棒性和泛化能力。 Sen Zhang,Jing Zhang,Dacheng Tao cs.CV

authors_text: Zhuo Li,Runqiu Xiao,Hangting Chen,Zhenduo Zhao,Zihan Zhang,Wenchao Wang
title: The HCCL System for the NIST SRE21
title_zh: NIST SRE21 的 HCCL 系统
abstract: This paper describes the systems developed by the HCCL team for the NIST 2021 speaker recognition evaluation (NIST SRE21).We first explore various state-of-the-art speaker embedding extractors combined with a novel circle loss to obtain discriminative deep speaker embeddings. Considering that cross-channel and cross-linguistic speaker recognition are the key challenges of SRE21, we introduce several techniques to reduce the cross-domain mismatch. Specifically, Codec and speech enhancement are directly applied to the raw speech to eliminate the codecs and the environment noise mismatch. We denote the methods that work directly on speech to eliminate the relatively explicit mismatches collectively as data adaptation methods. Experiments show that data adaption methods achieve 15\% improvements over our baseline. Furthermore, some popular back-ends domain adaptation algorithms are deployed on speaker embeddings to alleviate speaker performance degradation caused by the implicit mismatch. Score calibration is a major failure for us in SRE21. The reason is that score calibration with too many parameters easily lead to overfitting problems.
summary: This paper describes the systems developed by the HCCL team for the NIST 2021 speaker recognition evaluation (NIST SRE21).We first explore various state-of-the-art speaker embedding extractors combined with a novel circle loss to obtain discriminative deep speaker embeddings. Considering that cross-channel and cross-linguistic speaker recognition are the key challenges of SRE21, we introduce several techniques to reduce the cross-domain mismatch. Specifically, Codec and speech enhancement are directly applied to the raw speech to eliminate the codecs and the environment noise mismatch. We denote the methods that work directly on speech to eliminate the relatively explicit mismatches collectively as data adaptation methods. Experiments show that data adaption methods achieve 15\% improvements over our baseline. Furthermore, some popular back-ends domain adaptation algorithms are deployed on speaker embeddings to alleviate speaker performance degradation caused by the implicit mismatch. Score calibration is a major failure for us in SRE21. The reason is that score calibration with too many parameters easily lead to overfitting problems.
abstract_zh: 本文介绍了 HCCL 团队为 NIST 2021 说话人识别评估 (NIST SRE21) 开发的系统。我们首先探索各种最先进的说话人嵌入提取器与新颖的圆损失相结合，以获得有区别的深度说话人嵌入。考虑到跨通道和跨语言的说话人识别是 SRE21 的关键挑战，我们引入了几种技术来减少跨域不匹配。具体来说，编解码器和语音增强直接应用于原始语音，以消除编解码器和环境噪声的不匹配。我们将直接作用于语音以消除相对明确的不匹配的方法统称为数据适应方法。实验表明，数据适应方法比我们的基线提高了 15%。此外，一些流行的后端域自适应算法被部署在说话人嵌入上，以减轻由隐式不匹配引起的说话人性能下降。分数校准是我们在SRE21中的一个重大失败。原因是参数过多的分数校准容易导致过拟合问题。

AI.tldr: 本文介绍了HCCL团队为NIST 2021说话人识别评估（NIST SRE21）开发的系统，重点解决跨通道和跨语言识别的挑战。
AI.motivation: NIST SRE21的核心挑战是跨通道和跨语言说话人识别，需减少领域差异带来的性能下降。
AI.method: 结合先进说话人嵌入提取器与圆环损失函数，采用数据自适应方法（如编解码器和语音增强）消除显式失配，并部署后端领域自适应算法，但分数校准因过拟合失败。
AI.result: 数据自适应方法使基线提升15%，但分数校准因参数过多导致过拟合问题未能有效解决。
AI.conclusion: 数据自适应显著提升性能，但需进一步优化分数校准以避免过拟合，未来可探索更鲁棒的校准策略。

authors_text: Jeonghun Baek,Yusuke Matsui,Kiyoharu Aizawa
title: COO: Comic Onomatopoeia Dataset for Recognizing Arbitrary or Truncated Texts
title_zh: COO：用于识别任意或截断文本的漫画拟声词数据集
abstract: Recognizing irregular texts has been a challenging topic in text recognition. To encourage research on this topic, we provide a novel comic onomatopoeia dataset (COO), which consists of onomatopoeia texts in Japanese comics. COO has many arbitrary texts, such as extremely curved, partially shrunk texts, or arbitrarily placed texts. Furthermore, some texts are separated into several parts. Each part is a truncated text and is not meaningful by itself. These parts should be linked to represent the intended meaning. Thus, we propose a novel task that predicts the link between truncated texts. We conduct three tasks to detect the onomatopoeia region and capture its intended meaning: text detection, text recognition, and link prediction. Through extensive experiments, we analyze the characteristics of the COO. Our data and code are available at \url{https://github.com/ku21fan/COO-Comic-Onomatopoeia}.
summary: Recognizing irregular texts has been a challenging topic in text recognition. To encourage research on this topic, we provide a novel comic onomatopoeia dataset (COO), which consists of onomatopoeia texts in Japanese comics. COO has many arbitrary texts, such as extremely curved, partially shrunk texts, or arbitrarily placed texts. Furthermore, some texts are separated into several parts. Each part is a truncated text and is not meaningful by itself. These parts should be linked to represent the intended meaning. Thus, we propose a novel task that predicts the link between truncated texts. We conduct three tasks to detect the onomatopoeia region and capture its intended meaning: text detection, text recognition, and link prediction. Through extensive experiments, we analyze the characteristics of the COO. Our data and code are available at \url{https://github.com/ku21fan/COO-Comic-Onomatopoeia}.
abstract_zh: 识别不规则文本一直是文本识别中的一个具有挑战性的课题。为了鼓励这一主题的研究，我们提供了一个新颖的漫画拟声词数据集（COO），其中包含日本漫画中的拟声词文本。 COO 有许多任意文本，例如极度弯曲、部分收缩的文本或任意放置的文本。此外，有些文本被分成几个部分。每个部分都是被截断的文本，本身没有任何意义。这些部分应该链接起来以代表预期的含义。因此，我们提出了一项新任务来预测截断文本之间的链接。我们执行三项任务来检测拟声词区域并捕获其预期含义：文本检测、文本识别和链接预测。通过大量的实验，我们分析了COO的特征。我们的数据和代码可在 \url{https://github.com/ku21fan/COO-Comic-Onomatopoeia} 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一个名为COO的漫画拟声词数据集，用于识别任意形状或截断文本，并提出链接预测任务以解决分割文本的语义连贯性问题。
AI.motivation: 不规则文本识别存在挑战，现有方法难以处理漫画中弯曲、缩放或分割的文本。通过构建包含复杂文本形态的数据集，推动相关技术研究。
AI.method: 构建包含弯曲、缩放及分割文本的日本漫画拟声词数据集，设计文本检测、文本识别和链接预测三个任务，通过实验分析数据集特性并验证方法有效性。
AI.result: 实验表明现有方法在COO数据集上表现不足，提出的链接预测任务能有效提升分割文本的语义理解能力，数据集和代码已开源。
AI.conclusion: COO数据集为不规则文本识别提供了新基准，链接预测任务有效解决了分割文本的语义连贯性问题，推动了文本识别领域的研究进展。

search_text: COO: Comic Onomatopoeia Dataset for Recognizing Arbitrary or Truncated Texts COO：用于识别任意或截断文本的漫画拟声词数据集 Recognizing irregular texts has been a challenging topic in text recognition. To encourage research on this topic, we provide a novel comic onomatopoeia dataset (COO), which consists of onomatopoeia texts in Japanese comics. COO has many arbitrary texts, such as extremely curved, partially shrunk texts, or arbitrarily placed texts. Furthermore, some texts are separated into several parts. Each part is a truncated text and is not meaningful by itself. These parts should be linked to represent the intended meaning. Thus, we propose a novel task that predicts the link between truncated texts. We conduct three tasks to detect the onomatopoeia region and capture its intended meaning: text detection, text recognition, and link prediction. Through extensive experiments, we analyze the characteristics of the COO. Our data and code are available at \url{https://github.com/ku21fan/COO-Comic-Onomatopoeia}. 识别不规则文本一直是文本识别中的一个具有挑战性的课题。为了鼓励这一主题的研究，我们提供了一个新颖的漫画拟声词数据集（COO），其中包含日本漫画中的拟声词文本。 COO 有许多任意文本，例如极度弯曲、部分收缩的文本或任意放置的文本。此外，有些文本被分成几个部分。每个部分都是被截断的文本，本身没有任何意义。这些部分应该链接起来以代表预期的含义。因此，我们提出了一项新任务来预测截断文本之间的链接。我们执行三项任务来检测拟声词区域并捕获其预期含义：文本检测、文本识别和链接预测。通过大量的实验，我们分析了COO的特征。我们的数据和代码可在 \url{https://github.com/ku21fan/COO-Comic-Onomatopoeia} 获取。 本文提出一个名为COO的漫画拟声词数据集，用于识别任意形状或截断文本，并提出链接预测任务以解决分割文本的语义连贯性问题。 Jeonghun Baek,Yusuke Matsui,Kiyoharu Aizawa cs.CV

authors_text: Shi Hanyu,Wei Jiacheng,Wang Hao,Liu Fayao,Lin Guosheng
title: Learning Spatial and Temporal Variations for 4D Point Cloud Segmentation
title_zh: 学习 4D 点云分割的空间和时间变化
abstract: LiDAR-based 3D scene perception is a fundamental and important task for autonomous driving. Most state-of-the-art methods on LiDAR-based 3D recognition tasks focus on single frame 3D point cloud data, and the temporal information is ignored in those methods. We argue that the temporal information across the frames provides crucial knowledge for 3D scene perceptions, especially in the driving scenario. In this paper, we focus on spatial and temporal variations to better explore the temporal information across the 3D frames. We design a temporal variation-aware interpolation module and a temporal voxel-point refiner to capture the temporal variation in the 4D point cloud. The temporal variation-aware interpolation generates local features from the previous and current frames by capturing spatial coherence and temporal variation information. The temporal voxel-point refiner builds a temporal graph on the 3D point cloud sequences and captures the temporal variation with a graph convolution module. The temporal voxel-point refiner also transforms the coarse voxel-level predictions into fine point-level predictions. With our proposed modules, the new network TVSN achieves state-of-the-art performance on SemanticKITTI and SemantiPOSS. Specifically, our method achieves 52.5\% in mIoU (+5.5% against previous best approaches) on the multiple scan segmentation task on SemanticKITTI, and 63.0% on SemanticPOSS (+2.8% against previous best approaches).
summary: LiDAR-based 3D scene perception is a fundamental and important task for autonomous driving. Most state-of-the-art methods on LiDAR-based 3D recognition tasks focus on single frame 3D point cloud data, and the temporal information is ignored in those methods. We argue that the temporal information across the frames provides crucial knowledge for 3D scene perceptions, especially in the driving scenario. In this paper, we focus on spatial and temporal variations to better explore the temporal information across the 3D frames. We design a temporal variation-aware interpolation module and a temporal voxel-point refiner to capture the temporal variation in the 4D point cloud. The temporal variation-aware interpolation generates local features from the previous and current frames by capturing spatial coherence and temporal variation information. The temporal voxel-point refiner builds a temporal graph on the 3D point cloud sequences and captures the temporal variation with a graph convolution module. The temporal voxel-point refiner also transforms the coarse voxel-level predictions into fine point-level predictions. With our proposed modules, the new network TVSN achieves state-of-the-art performance on SemanticKITTI and SemantiPOSS. Specifically, our method achieves 52.5\% in mIoU (+5.5% against previous best approaches) on the multiple scan segmentation task on SemanticKITTI, and 63.0% on SemanticPOSS (+2.8% against previous best approaches).
abstract_zh: 基于激光雷达的3D场景感知是自动驾驶的一项基础且重要的任务。大多数基于 LiDAR 的 3D 识别任务的最先进方法都专注于单帧 3D 点云数据，而这些方法忽略了时间信息。我们认为，跨帧的时间信息为 3D 场景感知提供了至关重要的知识，尤其是在驾驶场景中。 In this paper, we focus on spatial and temporal variations to better explore the temporal information across the 3D frames.我们设计了一个时间变化感知插值模块和一个时间体素点细化器来捕获 4D 点云中的时间变化。时间变化感知插值通过捕获空间相干性和时间变化信息，从先前帧和当前帧生成局部特征。时间体素点细化器在 3D 点云序列上构建时间图，并使用图卷积模块捕获时间变化。 The temporal voxel-point refiner also transforms the coarse voxel-level predictions into fine point-level predictions.借助我们提出的模块，新网络 TVSN 在 SemanticKITTI 和 SemantiPOSS 上实现了最先进的性能。具体来说，我们的方法在 SemanticKITTI 上的多次扫描分割任务上达到了 52.5% 的 mIoU（比之前的最佳方法+5.5%），在 SemanticPOSS 上达到了 63.0%（比之前的最佳方法+2.8%）。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种用于4D点云分割的方法，通过学习空间和时间变化来提升LiDAR基于的3D场景感知性能。
AI.motivation: 现有基于LiDAR的3D识别方法忽视了帧间时间信息，而时间信息对驾驶场景中的动态感知至关重要。
AI.method: 设计时间变化感知插值模块（捕捉帧间空间连续性和时间变化）与时间体素-点细化模块（构建时序图卷积网络并优化点级预测）。
AI.result: 在SemanticKITTI数据集上多扫描分割任务mIoU达52.5%（+5.5%），SemanticPOSS数据集达63.0%（+2.8%）。
AI.conclusion: 通过显式建模时空变化，所提TVSN网络在4D点云分割任务中取得当前最优性能。

authors_text: NLLB Team,Marta R. Costa-jussà,James Cross,Onur Çelebi,Maha Elbayad,Kenneth Heafield,Kevin Heffernan,Elahe Kalbassi,Janice Lam,Daniel Licht,Jean Maillard,Anna Sun,Skyler Wang,Guillaume Wenzek,Al Youngblood,Bapi Akula,Loic Barrault,Gabriel Mejia Gonzalez,Prangthip Hansanti,John Hoffman,Semarley Jarrett,Kaushik Ram Sadagopan,Dirk Rowe,Shannon Spruit,Chau Tran, et al. (14 additional authors not shown)
title: No Language Left Behind: Scaling Human-Centered Machine Translation
title_zh: 不遗余力：扩展以人为本的机器翻译
abstract: Driven by the goal of eradicating language barriers on a global scale, machine translation has solidified itself as a key focus of artificial intelligence research today. However, such efforts have coalesced around a small subset of languages, leaving behind the vast majority of mostly low-resource languages. What does it take to break the 200 language barrier while ensuring safe, high quality results, all while keeping ethical considerations in mind? In No Language Left Behind, we took on this challenge by first contextualizing the need for low-resource language translation support through exploratory interviews with native speakers. Then, we created datasets and models aimed at narrowing the performance gap between low and high-resource languages. More specifically, we developed a conditional compute model based on Sparsely Gated Mixture of Experts that is trained on data obtained with novel and effective data mining techniques tailored for low-resource languages. We propose multiple architectural and training improvements to counteract overfitting while training on thousands of tasks. Critically, we evaluated the performance of over 40,000 different translation directions using a human-translated benchmark, Flores-200, and combined human evaluation with a novel toxicity benchmark covering all languages in Flores-200 to assess translation safety. Our model achieves an improvement of 44% BLEU relative to the previous state-of-the-art, laying important groundwork towards realizing a universal translation system. Finally, we open source all contributions described in this work, accessible at https://github.com/facebookresearch/fairseq/tree/nllb.
summary: Driven by the goal of eradicating language barriers on a global scale, machine translation has solidified itself as a key focus of artificial intelligence research today. However, such efforts have coalesced around a small subset of languages, leaving behind the vast majority of mostly low-resource languages. What does it take to break the 200 language barrier while ensuring safe, high quality results, all while keeping ethical considerations in mind? In No Language Left Behind, we took on this challenge by first contextualizing the need for low-resource language translation support through exploratory interviews with native speakers. Then, we created datasets and models aimed at narrowing the performance gap between low and high-resource languages. More specifically, we developed a conditional compute model based on Sparsely Gated Mixture of Experts that is trained on data obtained with novel and effective data mining techniques tailored for low-resource languages. We propose multiple architectural and training improvements to counteract overfitting while training on thousands of tasks. Critically, we evaluated the performance of over 40,000 different translation directions using a human-translated benchmark, Flores-200, and combined human evaluation with a novel toxicity benchmark covering all languages in Flores-200 to assess translation safety. Our model achieves an improvement of 44% BLEU relative to the previous state-of-the-art, laying important groundwork towards realizing a universal translation system. Finally, we open source all contributions described in this work, accessible at https://github.com/facebookresearch/fairseq/tree/nllb.
abstract_zh: 在消除全球范围内的语言障碍这一目标的推动下，机器翻译已成为当今人工智能研究的重点。然而，这些努力只集中在一小部分语言上，而留下了绝大多数资源匮乏的语言。如何才能打破 200 种语言障碍，同时确保安全、高质量的结果，同时牢记道德考虑？在《不让任何语言落后》中，我们首先通过对母语人士进行探索性访谈，了解对低资源语言翻译支持的需求，从而应对这一挑战。然后，我们创建了数据集和模型，旨在缩小低资源语言和高资源语言之间的性能差距。更具体地说，我们开发了一种基于稀疏门控混合专家的条件计算模型，该模型根据针对低资源语言量身定制的新颖有效的数据挖掘技术获得的数据进行训练。我们提出了多项架构和训练改进，以在对数千项任务进行训练时抵消过度拟合。至关重要的是，我们使用人工翻译基准 Flores-200 评估了 40,000 多个不同翻译方向的性能，并将人工评估与覆盖 Flores-200 中所有语言的新颖毒性基准相结合，以评估翻译安全性。与之前最先进的模型相比，我们的模型的 BLEU 提高了 44%，为实现通用翻译系统奠定了重要的基础。最后，我们开源了这项工作中描述的所有贡献，可以通过 https://github.com/facebookresearch/fairseq/tree/nllb 访问。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在开发一种面向所有语言（尤其是低资源语言）的高质量、安全的机器翻译系统，以消除全球语言障碍。
AI.motivation: 当前机器翻译研究主要集中在少数高资源语言上，忽视了大量低资源语言的需求，导致语言鸿沟扩大，需通过技术改进实现更公平的翻译支持。
AI.method: 通过与母语者访谈明确需求，构建低资源语言数据集，设计基于稀疏门控专家混合的条件计算模型，并采用新型数据挖掘技术；结合人类评估与毒性检测基准（Flores-200）进行多维度验证。
AI.result: 模型在40,000种翻译方向上实现BLEU分数较之前最优结果提升44%，并开源所有技术成果。
AI.conclusion: 该研究为构建覆盖200种语言的通用翻译系统奠定基础，通过技术创新与开源共享推动语言公平性目标的实现。

tldr: 本文旨在开发一种面向所有语言（尤其是低资源语言）的高质量、安全的机器翻译系统，以消除全球语言障碍。
motivation: 当前机器翻译研究主要集中在少数高资源语言上，忽视了大量低资源语言的需求，导致语言鸿沟扩大，需通过技术改进实现更公平的翻译支持。
method: 通过与母语者访谈明确需求，构建低资源语言数据集，设计基于稀疏门控专家混合的条件计算模型，并采用新型数据挖掘技术；结合人类评估与毒性检测基准（Flores-200）进行多维度验证。
result: 模型在40,000种翻译方向上实现BLEU分数较之前最优结果提升44%，并开源所有技术成果。
conclusion: 该研究为构建覆盖200种语言的通用翻译系统奠定基础，通过技术创新与开源共享推动语言公平性目标的实现。
search_text: No Language Left Behind: Scaling Human-Centered Machine Translation 不遗余力：扩展以人为本的机器翻译 Driven by the goal of eradicating language barriers on a global scale, machine translation has solidified itself as a key focus of artificial intelligence research today. However, such efforts have coalesced around a small subset of languages, leaving behind the vast majority of mostly low-resource languages. What does it take to break the 200 language barrier while ensuring safe, high quality results, all while keeping ethical considerations in mind? In No Language Left Behind, we took on this challenge by first contextualizing the need for low-resource language translation support through exploratory interviews with native speakers. Then, we created datasets and models aimed at narrowing the performance gap between low and high-resource languages. More specifically, we developed a conditional compute model based on Sparsely Gated Mixture of Experts that is trained on data obtained with novel and effective data mining techniques tailored for low-resource languages. We propose multiple architectural and training improvements to counteract overfitting while training on thousands of tasks. Critically, we evaluated the performance of over 40,000 different translation directions using a human-translated benchmark, Flores-200, and combined human evaluation with a novel toxicity benchmark covering all languages in Flores-200 to assess translation safety. Our model achieves an improvement of 44% BLEU relative to the previous state-of-the-art, laying important groundwork towards realizing a universal translation system. Finally, we open source all contributions described in this work, accessible at https://github.com/facebookresearch/fairseq/tree/nllb. 在消除全球范围内的语言障碍这一目标的推动下，机器翻译已成为当今人工智能研究的重点。然而，这些努力只集中在一小部分语言上，而留下了绝大多数资源匮乏的语言。如何才能打破 200 种语言障碍，同时确保安全、高质量的结果，同时牢记道德考虑？在《不让任何语言落后》中，我们首先通过对母语人士进行探索性访谈，了解对低资源语言翻译支持的需求，从而应对这一挑战。然后，我们创建了数据集和模型，旨在缩小低资源语言和高资源语言之间的性能差距。更具体地说，我们开发了一种基于稀疏门控混合专家的条件计算模型，该模型根据针对低资源语言量身定制的新颖有效的数据挖掘技术获得的数据进行训练。我们提出了多项架构和训练改进，以在对数千项任务进行训练时抵消过度拟合。至关重要的是，我们使用人工翻译基准 Flores-200 评估了 40,000 多个不同翻译方向的性能，并将人工评估与覆盖 Flores-200 中所有语言的新颖毒性基准相结合，以评估翻译安全性。与之前最先进的模型相比，我们的模型的 BLEU 提高了 44%，为实现通用翻译系统奠定了重要的基础。最后，我们开源了这项工作中描述的所有贡献，可以通过 https://github.com/facebookresearch/fairseq/tree/nllb 访问。 本文旨在开发一种面向所有语言（尤其是低资源语言）的高质量、安全的机器翻译系统，以消除全球语言障碍。 NLLB Team,Marta R. Costa-jussà,James Cross,Onur Çelebi,Maha Elbayad,Kenneth Heafield,Kevin Heffernan,Elahe Kalbassi,Janice Lam,Daniel Licht,Jean Maillard,Anna Sun,Skyler Wang,Guillaume Wenzek,Al Youngblood,Bapi Akula,Loic Barrault,Gabriel Mejia Gonzalez,Prangthip Hansanti,John Hoffman,Semarley Jarrett,Kaushik Ram Sadagopan,Dirk Rowe,Shannon Spruit,Chau Tran, et al. (14 additional authors not shown) cs.CL,cs.AI

authors_text: Kunran Xu,Huawei Zhang,Yishi Li,Yuhao Zhang,Rui Lai,Yi Liu
title: An Ultra-low Power TinyML System for Real-time Visual Processing at Edge
title_zh: 用于边缘实时视觉处理的超低功耗 TinyML 系统
abstract: Tiny machine learning (TinyML), executing AI workloads on resource and power strictly restricted systems, is an important and challenging topic. This brief firstly presents an extremely tiny backbone to construct high efficiency CNN models for various visual tasks. Then, a specially designed neural co-processor (NCP) is interconnected with MCU to build an ultra-low power TinyML system, which stores all features and weights on chip and completely removes both of latency and power consumption in off-chip memory access. Furthermore, an application specific instruction-set is further presented for realizing agile development and rapid deployment. Extensive experiments demonstrate that the proposed TinyML system based on our model, NCP and instruction set yields considerable accuracy and achieves a record ultra-low power of 160mW while implementing object detection and recognition at 30FPS. The demo video is available on \url{https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mIZPxtJ-9EY}.
summary: Tiny machine learning (TinyML), executing AI workloads on resource and power strictly restricted systems, is an important and challenging topic. This brief firstly presents an extremely tiny backbone to construct high efficiency CNN models for various visual tasks. Then, a specially designed neural co-processor (NCP) is interconnected with MCU to build an ultra-low power TinyML system, which stores all features and weights on chip and completely removes both of latency and power consumption in off-chip memory access. Furthermore, an application specific instruction-set is further presented for realizing agile development and rapid deployment. Extensive experiments demonstrate that the proposed TinyML system based on our model, NCP and instruction set yields considerable accuracy and achieves a record ultra-low power of 160mW while implementing object detection and recognition at 30FPS. The demo video is available on \url{https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mIZPxtJ-9EY}.
abstract_zh: 微型机器学习 (TinyML) 在资源和功率严格受限的系统上执行人工智能工作负载，是一个重要且具有挑战性的主题。本简介首先介绍了一个极其微小的主干网络，用于为各种视觉任务构建高效的 CNN 模型。然后，专门设计的神经协处理器（NCP）与MCU互连，构建超低功耗TinyML系统，该系统将所有特征和权重存储在芯片上，并完全消除片外存储器访问的延迟和功耗。此外，还进一步提出了应用专用指令集，以实现敏捷开发和快速部署。大量实验表明，所提出的基于我们的模型、NCP 和指令集的 TinyML 系统具有相当高的准确性，并在以 30FPS 实现目标检测和识别的同时实现了创纪录的 160mW 超低功耗。演示视频可在 \url{https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mIZPxtJ-9EY} 上获取。

AI.tldr: 开发一种超低功耗的TinyML系统，用于边缘设备的实时视觉处理。
AI.motivation: 解决在资源和功耗严格受限的系统中执行AI视觉任务的挑战，提高能效与实时性。
AI.method: 设计轻量级CNN骨干网络、神经协处理器（NCP）与MCU互联架构，并引入专用指令集以优化部署。
AI.result: 实现30FPS的实时目标检测与识别，功耗低至160mW，准确率显著。
AI.conclusion: 提出的系统在边缘视觉任务中兼顾高精度与超低功耗，适用于资源受限场景。

authors_text: Naoki Makishima,Satoshi Suzuki,Atsushi Ando,Ryo Masumura
title: Speaker consistency loss and step-wise optimization for semi-supervised joint training of TTS and ASR using unpaired text data
title_zh: 使用不成对文本数据进行 TTS 和 ASR 半监督联合训练的说话人一致性损失和逐步优化
abstract: In this paper, we investigate the semi-supervised joint training of text to speech (TTS) and automatic speech recognition (ASR), where a small amount of paired data and a large amount of unpaired text data are available. Conventional studies form a cycle called the TTS-ASR pipeline, where the multispeaker TTS model synthesizes speech from text with a reference speech and the ASR model reconstructs the text from the synthesized speech, after which both models are trained with a cycle-consistency loss. However, the synthesized speech does not reflect the speaker characteristics of the reference speech and the synthesized speech becomes overly easy for the ASR model to recognize after training. This not only decreases the TTS model quality but also limits the ASR model improvement. To solve this problem, we propose improving the cycleconsistency-based training with a speaker consistency loss and step-wise optimization. The speaker consistency loss brings the speaker characteristics of the synthesized speech closer to that of the reference speech. In the step-wise optimization, we first freeze the parameter of the TTS model before both models are trained to avoid over-adaptation of the TTS model to the ASR model. Experimental results demonstrate the efficacy of the proposed method.
summary: In this paper, we investigate the semi-supervised joint training of text to speech (TTS) and automatic speech recognition (ASR), where a small amount of paired data and a large amount of unpaired text data are available. Conventional studies form a cycle called the TTS-ASR pipeline, where the multispeaker TTS model synthesizes speech from text with a reference speech and the ASR model reconstructs the text from the synthesized speech, after which both models are trained with a cycle-consistency loss. However, the synthesized speech does not reflect the speaker characteristics of the reference speech and the synthesized speech becomes overly easy for the ASR model to recognize after training. This not only decreases the TTS model quality but also limits the ASR model improvement. To solve this problem, we propose improving the cycleconsistency-based training with a speaker consistency loss and step-wise optimization. The speaker consistency loss brings the speaker characteristics of the synthesized speech closer to that of the reference speech. In the step-wise optimization, we first freeze the parameter of the TTS model before both models are trained to avoid over-adaptation of the TTS model to the ASR model. Experimental results demonstrate the efficacy of the proposed method.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们研究了文本转语音（TTS）和自动语音识别（ASR）的半监督联合训练，其中有少量配对数据和大量未配对文本数据。传统研究形成一个称为 TTS-ASR 管道的循环，其中多说话者 TTS 模型从文本与参考语音合成语音，ASR 模型从合成语音重建文本，之后两个模型都使用循环一致性损失进行训练。然而，合成语音并不能反映参考语音的说话人特征，并且合成语音在训练后变得过于容易被ASR模型识别。这不仅降低了TTS模型的质量，而且限制了ASR模型的改进。为了解决这个问题，我们建议通过说话者一致性损失和逐步优化来改进基于循环一致性的训练。说话人一致性损失使合成语音的说话人特征更接近参考语音的说话人特征。在逐步优化中，我们首先在训练两个模型之前冻结TTS模型的参数，以避免TTS模型对ASR模型的过度适应。实验结果证明了该方法的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文研究了在少量配对数据和大量未配对文本数据条件下，通过引入说话人一致性损失和分步优化策略，改进TTS与ASR的半监督联合训练方法。
AI.motivation: 传统TTS-ASR循环训练中，合成语音无法反映参考语音的说话人特征，导致ASR模型识别难度降低，进而影响TTS质量与ASR性能提升。
AI.method: 提出结合说话人一致性损失（使合成语音贴近参考语音特征）与分步优化策略（先冻结TTS模型参数再联合训练，避免TTS过度适配ASR）的改进训练框架。
AI.result: 实验验证了所提方法在提升TTS语音质量与ASR识别准确率方面的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 通过说话人一致性约束与训练顺序优化，可有效解决半监督联合训练中TTS与ASR模型的性能瓶颈问题。

The speaker consistency loss brings the speaker characteristics of the synthesized speech closer to that of the reference speech. In the step-wise optimization, we first freeze the parameter of the TTS model before both models are trained to avoid over-adaptation of the TTS model to the ASR model. Experimental results demonstrate the efficacy of the proposed method. 在本文中，我们研究了文本转语音（TTS）和自动语音识别（ASR）的半监督联合训练，其中有少量配对数据和大量未配对文本数据。传统研究形成一个称为 TTS-ASR 管道的循环，其中多说话者 TTS 模型从文本与参考语音合成语音，ASR 模型从合成语音重建文本，之后两个模型都使用循环一致性损失进行训练。然而，合成语音并不能反映参考语音的说话人特征，并且合成语音在训练后变得过于容易被ASR模型识别。这不仅降低了TTS模型的质量，而且限制了ASR模型的改进。为了解决这个问题，我们建议通过说话者一致性损失和逐步优化来改进基于循环一致性的训练。说话人一致性损失使合成语音的说话人特征更接近参考语音的说话人特征。在逐步优化中，我们首先在训练两个模型之前冻结TTS模型的参数，以避免TTS模型对ASR模型的过度适应。实验结果证明了该方法的有效性。 本文研究了在少量配对数据和大量未配对文本数据条件下，通过引入说话人一致性损失和分步优化策略，改进TTS与ASR的半监督联合训练方法。 Naoki Makishima,Satoshi Suzuki,Atsushi Ando,Ryo Masumura cs.SD,eess.AS

authors_text: Jiacheng Wang,Yueming Jin,Liansheng Wang
title: Personalizing Federated Medical Image Segmentation via Local Calibration
title_zh: 通过本地校准个性化联合医学图像分割
abstract: Medical image segmentation under federated learning (FL) is a promising direction by allowing multiple clinical sites to collaboratively learn a global model without centralizing datasets. However, using a single model to adapt to various data distributions from different sites is extremely challenging. Personalized FL tackles this issue by only utilizing partial model parameters shared from global server, while keeping the rest to adapt to its own data distribution in the local training of each site. However, most existing methods concentrate on the partial parameter splitting, while do not consider the \textit{inter-site in-consistencies} during the local training, which in fact can facilitate the knowledge communication over sites to benefit the model learning for improving the local accuracy. In this paper, we propose a personalized federated framework with \textbf{L}ocal \textbf{C}alibration (LC-Fed), to leverage the inter-site in-consistencies in both \textit{feature- and prediction- levels} to boost the segmentation. Concretely, as each local site has its alternative attention on the various features, we first design the contrastive site embedding coupled with channel selection operation to calibrate the encoded features. Moreover, we propose to exploit the knowledge of prediction-level in-consistency to guide the personalized modeling on the ambiguous regions, e.g., anatomical boundaries. It is achieved by computing a disagreement-aware map to calibrate the prediction. Effectiveness of our method has been verified on three medical image segmentation tasks with different modalities, where our method consistently shows superior performance to the state-of-the-art personalized FL methods. Code is available at https://github.com/jcwang123/FedLC.
summary: Medical image segmentation under federated learning (FL) is a promising direction by allowing multiple clinical sites to collaboratively learn a global model without centralizing datasets. However, using a single model to adapt to various data distributions from different sites is extremely challenging. Personalized FL tackles this issue by only utilizing partial model parameters shared from global server, while keeping the rest to adapt to its own data distribution in the local training of each site. However, most existing methods concentrate on the partial parameter splitting, while do not consider the \textit{inter-site in-consistencies} during the local training, which in fact can facilitate the knowledge communication over sites to benefit the model learning for improving the local accuracy. In this paper, we propose a personalized federated framework with \textbf{L}ocal \textbf{C}alibration (LC-Fed), to leverage the inter-site in-consistencies in both \textit{feature- and prediction- levels} to boost the segmentation. Concretely, as each local site has its alternative attention on the various features, we first design the contrastive site embedding coupled with channel selection operation to calibrate the encoded features. Moreover, we propose to exploit the knowledge of prediction-level in-consistency to guide the personalized modeling on the ambiguous regions, e.g., anatomical boundaries. It is achieved by computing a disagreement-aware map to calibrate the prediction. Effectiveness of our method has been verified on three medical image segmentation tasks with different modalities, where our method consistently shows superior performance to the state-of-the-art personalized FL methods. Code is available at https://github.com/jcwang123/FedLC.
abstract_zh: 联邦学习（FL）下的医学图像分割是一个有前途的方向，它允许多个临床站点协作学习全局模型，而无需集中数据集。然而，使用单一模型来适应来自不同站点的各种数据分布极具挑战性。个性化 FL 通过仅​​利用从全局服务器共享的部分模型参数来解决这个问题，同时保留其余部分以适应每个站点本地训练中自己的数据分布。然而，大多数现有方法集中于部分参数分割，而没有考虑本地训练期间的\textit{站点间不一致性}，这实际上可以促进站点之间的知识交流，有利于模型学习，从而提高本地精度。在本文中，我们提出了一种具有\textbf{L}ocal \textbf{C}校准（LC-Fed）的个性化联合框架，以利用\textit{特征级别和预测级别}中的站点间不一致性来增强分割。具体来说，由于每个本地站点对各种特征都有不同的关注，因此我们首先设计对比站点嵌入和通道选择操作来校准编码特征。此外，我们建议利用预测级别不一致的知识来指导模糊区域（例如解剖边界）的个性化建模。它是通过计算分歧感知图来校准预测来实现的。我们的方法的有效性已经在不同模态的三个医学图像分割任务上得到了验证，其中我们的方法始终表现出优于最先进的个性化 FL 方法的性能。代码可在 https://github.com/jcwang123/FedLC 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种名为LC-Fed的个性化联邦学习框架，通过利用跨站点的特征级和预测级不一致性来提升医疗图像分割的性能。
AI.motivation: 现有个性化联邦学习方法仅关注参数分割，忽视了跨站点训练中的不一致性，而这些不一致性可促进知识共享并提升本地模型准确性。
AI.method: 设计对比站点嵌入与通道选择操作进行特征校准，并通过计算分歧感知图对预测结果进行校准，特别针对解剖边界等模糊区域进行个性化建模。
AI.result: 在三种不同模态的医学图像分割任务中，该方法持续优于现有最先进的个性化联邦学习方法。
AI.conclusion: 通过有效利用跨站点不一致性，LC-Fed框架在特征和预测层面实现了更精准的本地化校准，显著提升了分割性能。

authors_text: Lalita Kumari,Sukhdeep Singh,VVS Rathore,Anuj Sharma
title: A Lexicon and Depth-wise Separable Convolution Based Handwritten Text Recognition System
title_zh: 基于词典和深度可分离卷积的手写文本识别系统
abstract: Cursive handwritten text recognition is a challenging research problem in the domain of pattern recognition. The current state-of-the-art approaches include models based on convolutional recurrent neural networks and multi-dimensional long short-term memory recurrent neural networks techniques. These methods are highly computationally extensive as well model is complex at design level. In recent studies, combination of convolutional neural network and gated convolutional neural networks based models demonstrated less number of parameters in comparison to convolutional recurrent neural networks based models. In the direction to reduced the total number of parameters to be trained, in this work, we have used depthwise convolution in place of standard convolutions with a combination of gated-convolutional neural network and bidirectional gated recurrent unit to reduce the total number of parameters to be trained. Additionally, we have also included a lexicon based word beam search decoder at testing step. It also helps in improving the the overall accuracy of the model. We have obtained 3.84% character error rate and 9.40% word error rate on IAM dataset; 4.88% character error rate and 14.56% word error rate in George Washington dataset, respectively.
summary: Cursive handwritten text recognition is a challenging research problem in the domain of pattern recognition. The current state-of-the-art approaches include models based on convolutional recurrent neural networks and multi-dimensional long short-term memory recurrent neural networks techniques. These methods are highly computationally extensive as well model is complex at design level. In recent studies, combination of convolutional neural network and gated convolutional neural networks based models demonstrated less number of parameters in comparison to convolutional recurrent neural networks based models. In the direction to reduced the total number of parameters to be trained, in this work, we have used depthwise convolution in place of standard convolutions with a combination of gated-convolutional neural network and bidirectional gated recurrent unit to reduce the total number of parameters to be trained. Additionally, we have also included a lexicon based word beam search decoder at testing step. It also helps in improving the the overall accuracy of the model. We have obtained 3.84% character error rate and 9.40% word error rate on IAM dataset; 4.88% character error rate and 14.56% word error rate in George Washington dataset, respectively.
abstract_zh: 草书手写文本识别是模式识别领域中一个具有挑战性的研究问题。当前最先进的方法包括基于卷积递归神经网络和多维长短期记忆递归神经网络技术的模型。这些方法的计算量很大，而且模型在设计层面也很复杂。在最近的研究中，与基于卷积递归神经网络的模型相比，基于卷积神经网络和门控卷积神经网络的模型的组合显示出更少的参数数量。为了减少要训练的参数总数，在这项工作中，我们使用深度卷积代替标准卷积，并结合门控卷积神经网络和双向门控循环单元来减少要训练的参数总数。此外，我们还在测试步骤中包含了基于词典的词束搜索解码器。它还有助于提高模型的整体准确性。我们在 IAM 数据集上获得了 3.84% 的字符错误率和 9.40% 的单词错误率；乔治·华盛顿数据集中的字符错误率和单词错误率分别为 4.88% 和 14.56%。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种结合词典和深度可分离卷积的手写文本识别系统，旨在减少模型参数并提高识别准确率。
AI.motivation: 现有手写文本识别模型（如卷积循环神经网络）计算复杂度高且参数过多，需探索更高效的轻量级解决方案。
AI.method: 采用深度可分离卷积替代标准卷积，结合门控卷积神经网络与双向门控循环单元，并引入基于词典的词束搜索解码器。
AI.result: 在IAM数据集上达到3.84%字符错误率和9.40%词错误率，在George Washington数据集上为4.88%和14.56%。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过参数优化和解码策略改进，在保持较高准确率的同时降低了计算复杂度，适用于实际场景部署。

authors_text: Yanqing Liu,Ruiqing Xue,Lei He,Xu Tan,Sheng Zhao
title: DelightfulTTS 2: End-to-End Speech Synthesis with Adversarial Vector-Quantized Auto-Encoders
title_zh: DelightfulTTS 2：使用对抗性矢量量化自动编码器进行端到端语音合成
abstract: Current text to speech (TTS) systems usually leverage a cascaded acoustic model and vocoder pipeline with mel-spectrograms as the intermediate representations, which suffer from two limitations: 1) the acoustic model and vocoder are separately trained instead of jointly optimized, which incurs cascaded errors; 2) the intermediate speech representations (e.g., mel-spectrogram) are pre-designed and lose phase information, which are sub-optimal. To solve these problems, in this paper, we develop DelightfulTTS 2, a new end-to-end speech synthesis system with automatically learned speech representations and jointly optimized acoustic model and vocoder. Specifically, 1) we propose a new codec network based on vector-quantized auto-encoders with adversarial training (VQ-GAN) to extract intermediate frame-level speech representations (instead of traditional representations like mel-spectrograms) and reconstruct speech waveform; 2) we jointly optimize the acoustic model (based on DelightfulTTS) and the vocoder (the decoder of VQ-GAN), with an auxiliary loss on the acoustic model to predict intermediate speech representations. Experiments show that DelightfulTTS 2 achieves a CMOS gain +0.14 over DelightfulTTS, and more method analyses further verify the effectiveness of the developed system.
summary: Current text to speech (TTS) systems usually leverage a cascaded acoustic model and vocoder pipeline with mel-spectrograms as the intermediate representations, which suffer from two limitations: 1) the acoustic model and vocoder are separately trained instead of jointly optimized, which incurs cascaded errors; 2) the intermediate speech representations (e.g., mel-spectrogram) are pre-designed and lose phase information, which are sub-optimal. To solve these problems, in this paper, we develop DelightfulTTS 2, a new end-to-end speech synthesis system with automatically learned speech representations and jointly optimized acoustic model and vocoder. Specifically, 1) we propose a new codec network based on vector-quantized auto-encoders with adversarial training (VQ-GAN) to extract intermediate frame-level speech representations (instead of traditional representations like mel-spectrograms) and reconstruct speech waveform; 2) we jointly optimize the acoustic model (based on DelightfulTTS) and the vocoder (the decoder of VQ-GAN), with an auxiliary loss on the acoustic model to predict intermediate speech representations. Experiments show that DelightfulTTS 2 achieves a CMOS gain +0.14 over DelightfulTTS, and more method analyses further verify the effectiveness of the developed system.
abstract_zh: 当前的文本转语音（TTS）系统通常利用级联的声学模型和声码器管道，以梅尔谱图作为中间表示，这受到两个限制：1）声学模型和声码器是单独训练而不是联合优化，这会产生级联错误； 2) the intermediate speech representations (e.g., mel-spectrogram) are pre-designed and lose phase information, which are sub-optimal.为了解决这些问题，在本文中，我们开发了 DelightfulTTS 2，这是一种新的端到端语音合成系统，具有自动学习的语音表示以及联合优化的声学模型和声码器。具体来说，1）我们提出了一种基于矢量量化自动编码器和对抗训练（VQ-GAN）的新编解码器网络，以提取中间帧级语音表示（而不是梅尔频谱图等传统表示）并重建语音波形； 2）我们联合优化声学模型（基于DelightfulTTS）和声码器（VQ-GAN的解码器），在声学模型上使用辅助损失来预测中间语音表示。实验表明，DelightfulTTS 2比DelightfulTTS实现了+0.14的CMOS增益，更多的方法分析进一步验证了所开发系统的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种端到端的语音合成系统DelightfulTTS 2，通过对抗训练的向量量化自编码器（VQ-GAN）自动学习语音表示，并联合优化声学模型和声码器。
AI.motivation: 当前的文本到语音（TTS）系统通常使用级联的声学模型和声码器，中间使用梅尔频谱图表示语音，但存在两个问题：1）声学模型和声码器分别训练而非联合优化，导致级联误差；2）中间语音表示是预设计的，丢失了相位信息，不是最优的。
AI.method: 本文提出了一种新的编解码网络，基于对抗训练的向量量化自编码器（VQ-GAN），用于提取中间帧级语音表示并重建语音波形。同时，联合优化声学模型（基于DelightfulTTS）和声码器（VQ-GAN的解码器），并在声学模型上引入辅助损失以预测中间语音表示。
AI.result: 实验表明，DelightfulTTS 2在CMOS指标上比DelightfulTTS提升了+0.14，进一步的方法分析也验证了所开发系统的有效性。
AI.conclusion: DelightfulTTS 2通过自动学习语音表示和联合优化声学模型与声码器，有效解决了传统TTS系统中的问题，提升了语音合成的性能。
