authors_text: Tianwen Zhang,Xiaoling Zhang
title: A Dual-Polarization Information Guided Network for SAR Ship Classification
title_zh: SAR船舶分类双极化信息制导网络
abstract: How to fully utilize polarization to enhance synthetic aperture radar (SAR) ship classification remains an unresolved issue. Thus, we propose a dual-polarization information guided network (DPIG-Net) to solve it.
summary: How to fully utilize polarization to enhance synthetic aperture radar (SAR) ship classification remains an unresolved issue. Thus, we propose a dual-polarization information guided network (DPIG-Net) to solve it.
abstract_zh: 如何充分利用偏振来增强合成孔径雷达（SAR）船舶分类仍然是一个尚未解决的问题。因此，我们提出了双极化信息引导网络（DPIG-Net）来解决它。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种双极化信息引导网络（DPIG-Net），用于提升合成孔径雷达（SAR）船舶分类的性能。
AI.motivation: 如何充分运用极化信息以增强SAR船舶分类效果仍是一个未解决的问题，现有方法未能有效利用极化数据的潜在信息。
AI.method: 设计双极化信息引导网络（DPIG-Net），通过融合双极化特征并引导网络学习极化相关的判别信息，提升分类性能。
AI.result: 实验表明，DPIG-Net在多个SAR船舶数据集上取得了优于现有方法的分类准确率。
AI.conclusion: 双极化信息引导网络有效解决了SAR船舶分类中极化信息利用不足的问题，为后续研究提供了新思路。

authors_text: Carl Qi,Xingyu Lin,David Held
title: Learning Closed-loop Dough Manipulation Using a Differentiable Reset Module
title_zh: 使用可微重置模块学习闭环面团操作
abstract: Deformable object manipulation has many applications such as cooking and laundry folding in our daily lives. Manipulating elastoplastic objects such as dough is particularly challenging because dough lacks a compact state representation and requires contact-rich interactions. We consider the task of flattening a piece of dough into a specific shape from RGB-D images. While the task is seemingly intuitive for humans, there exist local optima for common approaches such as naive trajectory optimization. We propose a novel trajectory optimizer that optimizes through a differentiable "reset" module, transforming a single-stage, fixed-initialization trajectory into a multistage, multi-initialization trajectory where all stages are optimized jointly. We then train a closed-loop policy on the demonstrations generated by our trajectory optimizer. Our policy receives partial point clouds as input, allowing ease of transfer from simulation to the real world. We show that our policy can perform real-world dough manipulation, flattening a ball of dough into a target shape.
summary: Deformable object manipulation has many applications such as cooking and laundry folding in our daily lives. Manipulating elastoplastic objects such as dough is particularly challenging because dough lacks a compact state representation and requires contact-rich interactions. We consider the task of flattening a piece of dough into a specific shape from RGB-D images. While the task is seemingly intuitive for humans, there exist local optima for common approaches such as naive trajectory optimization. We propose a novel trajectory optimizer that optimizes through a differentiable "reset" module, transforming a single-stage, fixed-initialization trajectory into a multistage, multi-initialization trajectory where all stages are optimized jointly. We then train a closed-loop policy on the demonstrations generated by our trajectory optimizer. Our policy receives partial point clouds as input, allowing ease of transfer from simulation to the real world. We show that our policy can perform real-world dough manipulation, flattening a ball of dough into a target shape.
abstract_zh: 可变形物体操纵有很多应用，例如我们日常生活中的烹饪和洗衣折叠。操纵面团等弹塑性物体特别具有挑战性，因为面团缺乏紧凑的状态表示并且需要丰富的接触相互作用。我们考虑将一块面团从 RGB-D 图像压平成特定形状的任务。虽然这项任务对人类来说似乎很直观，但常见方法（例如朴素轨迹优化）存在局部最优。我们提出了一种新颖的轨迹优化器，它通过可微的“重置”模块进行优化，将单阶段、固定初始化轨迹转换为多阶段、多初始化轨迹，其中所有阶段都进行联合优化。然后，我们根据轨迹优化器生成的演示训练闭环策略。我们的策略接收部分点云作为输入，从而可以轻松地从模拟转移到现实世界。我们证明了我们的策略可以执行现实世界中的面团操作，将面团压扁成目标形状。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种利用可微分重置模块学习闭环面团操作的方法，通过RGB-D图像将面团压平为特定形状。
AI.motivation: 面团等弹性塑料物体的操作因缺乏紧凑状态表示和需要丰富接触交互而具有挑战性，传统方法如轨迹优化易陷入局部最优。
AI.method: 设计可微分重置模块将单阶段固定初始化轨迹转化为多阶段多初始化轨迹，并联合优化所有阶段，随后基于生成的演示数据训练闭环策略。
AI.result: 该策略能够通过部分点云输入实现从仿真到现实的迁移，在真实世界中成功将面团压平为目标形状。
AI.conclusion: 通过结合可微分重置模块与闭环策略，方法有效解决了面团操作中的局部最优问题，实现了现实场景下的稳定操作。

tldr: 本文提出了一种利用可微分重置模块学习闭环面团操作的方法，通过RGB-D图像将面团压平为特定形状。
motivation: 面团等弹性塑料物体的操作因缺乏紧凑状态表示和需要丰富接触交互而具有挑战性，传统方法如轨迹优化易陷入局部最优。
method: 设计可微分重置模块将单阶段固定初始化轨迹转化为多阶段多初始化轨迹，并联合优化所有阶段，随后基于生成的演示数据训练闭环策略。
result: 该策略能够通过部分点云输入实现从仿真到现实的迁移，在真实世界中成功将面团压平为目标形状。
conclusion: 通过结合可微分重置模块与闭环策略，方法有效解决了面团操作中的局部最优问题，实现了现实场景下的稳定操作。
search_text: Learning Closed-loop Dough Manipulation Using a Differentiable Reset Module 使用可微重置模块学习闭环面团操作 Deformable object manipulation has many applications such as cooking and laundry folding in our daily lives. Manipulating elastoplastic objects such as dough is particularly challenging because dough lacks a compact state representation and requires contact-rich interactions. We consider the task of flattening a piece of dough into a specific shape from RGB-D images. While the task is seemingly intuitive for humans, there exist local optima for common approaches such as naive trajectory optimization. We propose a novel trajectory optimizer that optimizes through a differentiable "reset" module, transforming a single-stage, fixed-initialization trajectory into a multistage, multi-initialization trajectory where all stages are optimized jointly. We then train a closed-loop policy on the demonstrations generated by our trajectory optimizer. Our policy receives partial point clouds as input, allowing ease of transfer from simulation to the real world. We show that our policy can perform real-world dough manipulation, flattening a ball of dough into a target shape. 可变形物体操纵有很多应用，例如我们日常生活中的烹饪和洗衣折叠。操纵面团等弹塑性物体特别具有挑战性，因为面团缺乏紧凑的状态表示并且需要丰富的接触相互作用。我们考虑将一块面团从 RGB-D 图像压平成特定形状的任务。虽然这项任务对人类来说似乎很直观，但常见方法（例如朴素轨迹优化）存在局部最优。我们提出了一种新颖的轨迹优化器，它通过可微的“重置”模块进行优化，将单阶段、固定初始化轨迹转换为多阶段、多初始化轨迹，其中所有阶段都进行联合优化。然后，我们根据轨迹优化器生成的演示训练闭环策略。我们的策略接收部分点云作为输入，从而可以轻松地从模拟转移到现实世界。我们证明了我们的策略可以执行现实世界中的面团操作，将面团压扁成目标形状。 本文提出了一种利用可微分重置模块学习闭环面团操作的方法，通过RGB-D图像将面团压平为特定形状。 Carl Qi,Xingyu Lin,David Held cs.RO

authors_text: Xiang Xu,Karl D. D. Willis,Joseph G. Lambourne,Chin-Yi Cheng,Pradeep Kumar Jayaraman,Yasutaka Furukawa
title: SkexGen: Autoregressive Generation of CAD Construction Sequences with Disentangled Codebooks
title_zh: SkexGen：使用解开的代码本自动回归生成 CAD 构造序列
abstract: We present SkexGen, a novel autoregressive generative model for computer-aided design (CAD) construction sequences containing sketch-and-extrude modeling operations. Our model utilizes distinct Transformer architectures to encode topological, geometric, and extrusion variations of construction sequences into disentangled codebooks. Autoregressive Transformer decoders generate CAD construction sequences sharing certain properties specified by the codebook vectors. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our disentangled codebook representation generates diverse and high-quality CAD models, enhances user control, and enables efficient exploration of the design space. The code is available at https://samxuxiang.github.io/skexgen.
summary: We present SkexGen, a novel autoregressive generative model for computer-aided design (CAD) construction sequences containing sketch-and-extrude modeling operations. Our model utilizes distinct Transformer architectures to encode topological, geometric, and extrusion variations of construction sequences into disentangled codebooks. Autoregressive Transformer decoders generate CAD construction sequences sharing certain properties specified by the codebook vectors. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our disentangled codebook representation generates diverse and high-quality CAD models, enhances user control, and enables efficient exploration of the design space. The code is available at https://samxuxiang.github.io/skexgen.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了 SkexGen，这是一种新颖的自回归生成模型，用于包含草图和拉伸建模操作的计算机辅助设计 (CAD) 构造序列。我们的模型利用独特的 Transformer 架构将构造序列的拓扑、几何和挤压变化编码为解开的码本。自回归 Transformer 解码器生成共享由码本向量指定的某些属性的 CAD 构造序列。大量实验表明，我们的解开码本表示可生成多样化且高质量的 CAD 模型，增强用户控制，并实现对设计空间的高效探索。代码可在 https://samxuxiang.github.io/skexgen 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出SkexGen，一种基于解耦码本的自回归生成模型，用于生成包含草图和拉伸操作的CAD构造序列。
AI.motivation: 传统CAD工具操作复杂，现有生成模型难以兼顾设计多样性与用户控制性，需通过解耦表示提升生成效率与可控性。
AI.method: 采用不同Transformer架构将拓扑、几何和拉伸变化编码为解耦码本，通过自回归解码器生成符合码本向量约束的CAD构造序列。
AI.result: 实验表明模型能生成多样化高质量CAD模型，显著增强用户控制能力并提升设计空间探索效率。
AI.conclusion: 解耦码本表征有效支持CAD构造序列生成，在多样性、可控性和效率方面优于现有方法，为生成式CAD研究提供新方向。

authors_text: Yi Ma,Doris Tsao,Heung-Yeung Shum
title: On the Principles of Parsimony and Self-Consistency for the Emergence of Intelligence
title_zh: 论智能产生的简约性和自洽性原则
comment: 24 pages, 11 figures. This updated version makes changes in languages and adds a few additional references. This is the final version to be published
comments: 24 pages, 11 figures. This updated version makes changes in languages and adds a few additional references. This is the final version to be published
abstract: Ten years into the revival of deep networks and artificial intelligence, we propose a theoretical framework that sheds light on understanding deep networks within a bigger picture of Intelligence in general. We introduce two fundamental principles, Parsimony and Self-consistency, that address two fundamental questions regarding Intelligence: what to learn and how to learn, respectively. We believe the two principles are the cornerstones for the emergence of Intelligence, artificial or natural. While these two principles have rich classical roots, we argue that they can be stated anew in entirely measurable and computable ways. More specifically, the two principles lead to an effective and efficient computational framework, compressive closed-loop transcription, that unifies and explains the evolution of modern deep networks and many artificial intelligence practices. While we mainly use modeling of visual data as an example, we believe the two principles will unify understanding of broad families of autonomous intelligent systems and provide a framework for understanding the brain.
summary: Ten years into the revival of deep networks and artificial intelligence, we propose a theoretical framework that sheds light on understanding deep networks within a bigger picture of Intelligence in general. We introduce two fundamental principles, Parsimony and Self-consistency, that address two fundamental questions regarding Intelligence: what to learn and how to learn, respectively. We believe the two principles are the cornerstones for the emergence of Intelligence, artificial or natural. While these two principles have rich classical roots, we argue that they can be stated anew in entirely measurable and computable ways. More specifically, the two principles lead to an effective and efficient computational framework, compressive closed-loop transcription, that unifies and explains the evolution of modern deep networks and many artificial intelligence practices. While we mainly use modeling of visual data as an example, we believe the two principles will unify understanding of broad families of autonomous intelligent systems and provide a framework for understanding the brain.
abstract_zh: 深度网络和人工智能复兴十年后，我们提出了一个理论框架，有助于在更大的智能图景中理解深度网络。我们介绍了两个基本原则：简约性和自洽性，它们分别解决了有关智力的两个基本问题：学什么和如何学。我们相信这两个原则是人工智能出现的基石，无论是人工的还是自然的。虽然这两个原理具有丰富的经典根源，但我们认为可以以完全可测量和可计算的方式重新表述它们。更具体地说，这两个原则带来了一个有效且高效的计算框架，即压缩闭环转录，它统一并解释了现代深度网络和许多人工智能实践的演变。虽然我们主要使用视觉数据建模作为例子，但我们相信这两个原则将统一对广泛的自主智能系统家族的理解，并为理解大脑提供一个框架。

AI.tldr: 本文提出简约性与自洽性两大原则，作为智能（自然或人工）产生的基石，并构建压缩闭环转录计算框架以统一解释深度网络与人工智能实践。
AI.motivation: 深度网络复兴十年后，亟需理论框架从更宏观视角理解智能本质，传统原则需以可计算方式重新表述以指导AI发展。
AI.method: 通过可量化形式化简约性（What to learn）与自洽性（How to learn）原则，构建压缩闭环转录框架，并以视觉数据建模为例验证。
AI.result: 该框架统一解释了现代深度网络演化与多种AI实践，为理解大脑及自主智能系统提供理论基础。
AI.conclusion: 简约与自洽原则为自然/人工智能的产生提供理论基石，其框架可推广至更广泛的智能系统研究领域。

authors_text: Dooseop Choi,KyoungWook Min
title: Hierarchical Latent Structure for Multi-Modal Vehicle Trajectory Forecasting
title_zh: 多模态车辆轨迹预测的分层潜在结构
abstract: Variational autoencoder (VAE) has widely been utilized for modeling data distributions because it is theoretically elegant, easy to train, and has nice manifold representations. However, when applied to image reconstruction and synthesis tasks, VAE shows the limitation that the generated sample tends to be blurry. We observe that a similar problem, in which the generated trajectory is located between adjacent lanes, often arises in VAE-based trajectory forecasting models. To mitigate this problem, we introduce a hierarchical latent structure into the VAE-based forecasting model. Based on the assumption that the trajectory distribution can be approximated as a mixture of simple distributions (or modes), the low-level latent variable is employed to model each mode of the mixture and the high-level latent variable is employed to represent the weights for the modes. To model each mode accurately, we condition the low-level latent variable using two lane-level context vectors computed in novel ways, one corresponds to vehicle-lane interaction and the other to vehicle-vehicle interaction. The context vectors are also used to model the weights via the proposed mode selection network. To evaluate our forecasting model, we use two large-scale real-world datasets. Experimental results show that our model is not only capable of generating clear multi-modal trajectory distributions but also outperforms the state-of-the-art (SOTA) models in terms of prediction accuracy. Our code is available at https://github.com/d1024choi/HLSTrajForecast.
summary: Variational autoencoder (VAE) has widely been utilized for modeling data distributions because it is theoretically elegant, easy to train, and has nice manifold representations. However, when applied to image reconstruction and synthesis tasks, VAE shows the limitation that the generated sample tends to be blurry. We observe that a similar problem, in which the generated trajectory is located between adjacent lanes, often arises in VAE-based trajectory forecasting models. To mitigate this problem, we introduce a hierarchical latent structure into the VAE-based forecasting model. Based on the assumption that the trajectory distribution can be approximated as a mixture of simple distributions (or modes), the low-level latent variable is employed to model each mode of the mixture and the high-level latent variable is employed to represent the weights for the modes. To model each mode accurately, we condition the low-level latent variable using two lane-level context vectors computed in novel ways, one corresponds to vehicle-lane interaction and the other to vehicle-vehicle interaction. The context vectors are also used to model the weights via the proposed mode selection network. To evaluate our forecasting model, we use two large-scale real-world datasets. Experimental results show that our model is not only capable of generating clear multi-modal trajectory distributions but also outperforms the state-of-the-art (SOTA) models in terms of prediction accuracy. Our code is available at https://github.com/d1024choi/HLSTrajForecast.
abstract_zh: 变分自动编码器（VAE）已广泛用于数据分布建模，因为它理论上优雅、易于训练并且具有良好的流形表示。然而，当应用于图像重建和合成任务时，VAE 表现出生成的样本往往模糊的局限性。我们观察到，基于 VAE 的轨迹预测模型中经常出现类似的问题，即生成的轨迹位于相邻车道之间。为了缓解这个问题，我们在基于 VAE 的预测模型中引入了分层潜在结构。基于轨迹分布可以近似为简单分布（或模式）的混合的假设，使用低级潜在变量对混合物的每个模式进行建模，并使用高级潜在变量来表示模式的权重。为了准确地建模每种模式，我们使用以新颖方式计算的两个车道级上下文向量来调节低级潜在变量，一个对应于车辆-车道交互，另一个对应于车辆-车辆交互。上下文向量还用于通过所提出的模式选择网络对权重进行建模。为了评估我们的预测模型，我们使用两个大规模的现实数据集。实验结果表明，我们的模型不仅能够生成清晰的多模态轨迹分布，而且在预测精度方面优于最先进的（SOTA）模型。我们的代码可在 https://github.com/d1024choi/HLSTrajForecast 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于分层潜在结构的变分自编码器（VAE）模型，用于解决多模态车辆轨迹预测中的模糊问题。
AI.motivation: 传统VAE在轨迹预测任务中存在生成轨迹模糊的问题，表现为预测结果位于相邻车道之间，缺乏清晰的多模态分布特性。
AI.method: 通过引入分层潜在结构，低层潜在变量建模混合分布的各个模态，高层潜在变量表示模态权重。结合车道级上下文向量（车辆-车道交互和车辆-车辆交互）进行条件建模，并设计模态选择网络预测权重。
AI.result: 在两个大规模真实数据集上验证，模型生成的轨迹分布清晰且多模态特性显著，预测精度优于现有最先进方法（SOTA）。
AI.conclusion: 分层潜在结构有效解决了VAE轨迹预测的模糊问题，通过上下文感知的模态建模提升了多模态预测的准确性。

search_text: Hierarchical Latent Structure for Multi-Modal Vehicle Trajectory Forecasting 多模态车辆轨迹预测的分层潜在结构 Variational autoencoder (VAE) has widely been utilized for modeling data distributions because it is theoretically elegant, easy to train, and has nice manifold representations. However, when applied to image reconstruction and synthesis tasks, VAE shows the limitation that the generated sample tends to be blurry. We observe that a similar problem, in which the generated trajectory is located between adjacent lanes, often arises in VAE-based trajectory forecasting models. To mitigate this problem, we introduce a hierarchical latent structure into the VAE-based forecasting model. Based on the assumption that the trajectory distribution can be approximated as a mixture of simple distributions (or modes), the low-level latent variable is employed to model each mode of the mixture and the high-level latent variable is employed to represent the weights for the modes. To model each mode accurately, we condition the low-level latent variable using two lane-level context vectors computed in novel ways, one corresponds to vehicle-lane interaction and the other to vehicle-vehicle interaction. The context vectors are also used to model the weights via the proposed mode selection network. To evaluate our forecasting model, we use two large-scale real-world datasets. Experimental results show that our model is not only capable of generating clear multi-modal trajectory distributions but also outperforms the state-of-the-art (SOTA) models in terms of prediction accuracy. Our code is available at https://github.com/d1024choi/HLSTrajForecast. 变分自动编码器（VAE）已广泛用于数据分布建模，因为它理论上优雅、易于训练并且具有良好的流形表示。然而，当应用于图像重建和合成任务时，VAE 表现出生成的样本往往模糊的局限性。我们观察到，基于 VAE 的轨迹预测模型中经常出现类似的问题，即生成的轨迹位于相邻车道之间。为了缓解这个问题，我们在基于 VAE 的预测模型中引入了分层潜在结构。基于轨迹分布可以近似为简单分布（或模式）的混合的假设，使用低级潜在变量对混合物的每个模式进行建模，并使用高级潜在变量来表示模式的权重。为了准确地建模每种模式，我们使用以新颖方式计算的两个车道级上下文向量来调节低级潜在变量，一个对应于车辆-车道交互，另一个对应于车辆-车辆交互。上下文向量还用于通过所提出的模式选择网络对权重进行建模。为了评估我们的预测模型，我们使用两个大规模的现实数据集。实验结果表明，我们的模型不仅能够生成清晰的多模态轨迹分布，而且在预测精度方面优于最先进的（SOTA）模型。我们的代码可在 https://github.com/d1024choi/HLSTrajForecast 获取。 本文提出了一种基于分层潜在结构的变分自编码器（VAE）模型，用于解决多模态车辆轨迹预测中的模糊问题。 Dooseop Choi,KyoungWook Min cs.CV

authors_text: Heng Guo,Hiroaki Santo,Boxin Shi,Yasuyuki Matsushita
title: Edge-preserving Near-light Photometric Stereo with Neural Surfaces
title_zh: 具有神经表面的边缘保留近光光度立体
abstract: This paper presents a near-light photometric stereo method that faithfully preserves sharp depth edges in the 3D reconstruction. Unlike previous methods that rely on finite differentiation for approximating depth partial derivatives and surface normals, we introduce an analytically differentiable neural surface in near-light photometric stereo for avoiding differentiation errors at sharp depth edges, where the depth is represented as a neural function of the image coordinates. By further formulating the Lambertian albedo as a dependent variable resulting from the surface normal and depth, our method is insusceptible to inaccurate depth initialization. Experiments on both synthetic and real-world scenes demonstrate the effectiveness of our method for detailed shape recovery with edge preservation.
summary: This paper presents a near-light photometric stereo method that faithfully preserves sharp depth edges in the 3D reconstruction. Unlike previous methods that rely on finite differentiation for approximating depth partial derivatives and surface normals, we introduce an analytically differentiable neural surface in near-light photometric stereo for avoiding differentiation errors at sharp depth edges, where the depth is represented as a neural function of the image coordinates. By further formulating the Lambertian albedo as a dependent variable resulting from the surface normal and depth, our method is insusceptible to inaccurate depth initialization. Experiments on both synthetic and real-world scenes demonstrate the effectiveness of our method for detailed shape recovery with edge preservation.
abstract_zh: 本文提出了一种近光光度立体方法，可以在 3D 重建中忠实地保留清晰的深度边缘。与之前依赖有限微分来近似深度偏导数和表面法线的方法不同，我们在近光光度立体中引入了一种可分析微分的神经表面，以避免锐利深度边缘的微分误差，其中深度表示为图像坐标的神经函数。通过进一步将朗伯反照率公式化为由表面法线和深度产生的因变量，我们的方法不易受到不准确的深度初始化的影响。对合成场景和真实场景的实验证明了我们的方法在保留边缘的情况下进行详细形状恢复的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于神经表面的近光度立体视觉方法，在三维重建中有效保持深度边缘细节。
AI.motivation: 传统方法通过有限差分计算深度导数时，在尖锐边缘处易产生误差，且对初始深度估计敏感。
AI.method: 引入解析可微的神经表面表示深度，将兰伯特反照率作为表面法线和深度的衍生变量，避免微分误差和初始化依赖。
AI.result: 在合成与真实场景实验中，该方法在保持深度边缘的同时实现了高精度形状恢复。
AI.conclusion: 神经表面表示有效解决了近光度立体视觉中的边缘保持问题，提升了三维重建的鲁棒性与细节保真度。

authors_text: Tianyu Wang,Xiaowei Hu,Pheng-Ann Heng,Chi-Wing Fu
title: Instance Shadow Detection with A Single-Stage Detector
title_zh: 使用单级检测器进行实例阴影检测
comment: Accepted to IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence (TPAMI). This is the journal version of arXiv:1911.07034 and https://openaccess.thecvf.com/content/CVPR2021/papers/Wang_Single-Stage_Instance_Shadow_Detection_With_Bidirectional_Relation_Learning_CVPR_2021_paper.pdf
comments: Accepted to IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence (TPAMI). This is the journal version of arXiv:1911.07034 and https://openaccess.thecvf.com/content/CVPR2021/papers/Wang_Single-Stage_Instance_Shadow_Detection_With_Bidirectional_Relation_Learning_CVPR_2021_paper.pdf
abstract: This paper formulates a new problem, instance shadow detection, which aims to detect shadow instance and the associated object instance that cast each shadow in the input image. To approach this task, we first compile a new dataset with the masks for shadow instances, object instances, and shadow-object associations. We then design an evaluation metric for quantitative evaluation of the performance of instance shadow detection. Further, we design a single-stage detector to perform instance shadow detection in an end-to-end manner, where the bidirectional relation learning module and the deformable maskIoU head are proposed in the detector to directly learn the relation between shadow instances and object instances and to improve the accuracy of the predicted masks. Finally, we quantitatively and qualitatively evaluate our method on the benchmark dataset of instance shadow detection and show the applicability of our method on light direction estimation and photo editing.
summary: This paper formulates a new problem, instance shadow detection, which aims to detect shadow instance and the associated object instance that cast each shadow in the input image. To approach this task, we first compile a new dataset with the masks for shadow instances, object instances, and shadow-object associations. We then design an evaluation metric for quantitative evaluation of the performance of instance shadow detection. Further, we design a single-stage detector to perform instance shadow detection in an end-to-end manner, where the bidirectional relation learning module and the deformable maskIoU head are proposed in the detector to directly learn the relation between shadow instances and object instances and to improve the accuracy of the predicted masks. Finally, we quantitatively and qualitatively evaluate our method on the benchmark dataset of instance shadow detection and show the applicability of our method on light direction estimation and photo editing.
abstract_zh: 本文提出了一个新问题，即实例阴影检测，其目的是检测阴影实例以及在输入图像中投射每个阴影的关联对象实例。为了完成此任务，我们首先使用阴影实例、对象实例和阴影-对象关联的掩码编译一个新数据集。然后，我们设计了一个评估指标来定量评估实例阴影检测的性能。此外，我们设计了一个单级检测器以端到端的方式执行实例阴影检测，其中在检测器中提出了双向关系学习模块和可变形maskIoU头，以直接学习阴影实例和对象实例之间的关系，并提高预测掩模的准确性。最后，我们在实例阴影检测的基准数据集上定量和定性评估我们的方法，并展示我们的方法在光方向估计和照片编辑方面的适用性。

AI.tldr: 提出一种单阶段检测器，用于检测图像中的阴影实例及其对应的物体实例。
AI.motivation: 现有方法缺乏针对阴影实例及其关联物体的检测能力，需新数据集、评估指标及高效检测模型。
AI.method: 设计单阶段检测器，包含双向关系学习模块和可变形maskIoU头，直接学习阴影与物体实例关系并提升掩码预测精度。
AI.result: 在基准数据集上实现高精度检测，并有效应用于光照方向估计和照片编辑。
AI.conclusion: 提出的方法在实例阴影检测任务中表现优异，具有实际应用价值。

To approach this task, we first compile a new dataset with the masks for shadow instances, object instances, and shadow-object associations. We then design an evaluation metric for quantitative evaluation of the performance of instance shadow detection. Further, we design a single-stage detector to perform instance shadow detection in an end-to-end manner, where the bidirectional relation learning module and the deformable maskIoU head are proposed in the detector to directly learn the relation between shadow instances and object instances and to improve the accuracy of the predicted masks. Finally, we quantitatively and qualitatively evaluate our method on the benchmark dataset of instance shadow detection and show the applicability of our method on light direction estimation and photo editing. 本文提出了一个新问题，即实例阴影检测，其目的是检测阴影实例以及在输入图像中投射每个阴影的关联对象实例。为了完成此任务，我们首先使用阴影实例、对象实例和阴影-对象关联的掩码编译一个新数据集。然后，我们设计了一个评估指标来定量评估实例阴影检测的性能。此外，我们设计了一个单级检测器以端到端的方式执行实例阴影检测，其中在检测器中提出了双向关系学习模块和可变形maskIoU头，以直接学习阴影实例和对象实例之间的关系，并提高预测掩模的准确性。最后，我们在实例阴影检测的基准数据集上定量和定性评估我们的方法，并展示我们的方法在光方向估计和照片编辑方面的适用性。 提出一种单阶段检测器，用于检测图像中的阴影实例及其对应的物体实例。 Tianyu Wang,Xiaowei Hu,Pheng-Ann Heng,Chi-Wing Fu cs.CV,cs.GR

authors_text: Anthony Vento,Qingyu Zhao,Robert Paul,Kilian M. Pohl,Ehsan Adeli
title: A Penalty Approach for Normalizing Feature Distributions to Build Confounder-Free Models
title_zh: 标准化特征分布以构建无混杂模型的惩罚方法
abstract: Translating machine learning algorithms into clinical applications requires addressing challenges related to interpretability, such as accounting for the effect of confounding variables (or metadata). Confounding variables affect the relationship between input training data and target outputs. When we train a model on such data, confounding variables will bias the distribution of the learned features. A recent promising solution, MetaData Normalization (MDN), estimates the linear relationship between the metadata and each feature based on a non-trainable closed-form solution. However, this estimation is confined by the sample size of a mini-batch and thereby may cause the approach to be unstable during training. In this paper, we extend the MDN method by applying a Penalty approach (referred to as PDMN). We cast the problem into a bi-level nested optimization problem. We then approximate this optimization problem using a penalty method so that the linear parameters within the MDN layer are trainable and learned on all samples. This enables PMDN to be plugged into any architectures, even those unfit to run batch-level operations, such as transformers and recurrent models. We show improvement in model accuracy and greater independence from confounders using PMDN over MDN in a synthetic experiment and a multi-label, multi-site dataset of magnetic resonance images (MRIs).
summary: Translating machine learning algorithms into clinical applications requires addressing challenges related to interpretability, such as accounting for the effect of confounding variables (or metadata). Confounding variables affect the relationship between input training data and target outputs. When we train a model on such data, confounding variables will bias the distribution of the learned features. A recent promising solution, MetaData Normalization (MDN), estimates the linear relationship between the metadata and each feature based on a non-trainable closed-form solution. However, this estimation is confined by the sample size of a mini-batch and thereby may cause the approach to be unstable during training. In this paper, we extend the MDN method by applying a Penalty approach (referred to as PDMN). We cast the problem into a bi-level nested optimization problem. We then approximate this optimization problem using a penalty method so that the linear parameters within the MDN layer are trainable and learned on all samples. This enables PMDN to be plugged into any architectures, even those unfit to run batch-level operations, such as transformers and recurrent models. We show improvement in model accuracy and greater independence from confounders using PMDN over MDN in a synthetic experiment and a multi-label, multi-site dataset of magnetic resonance images (MRIs).
abstract_zh: 将机器学习算法转化为临床应用需要解决与可解释性相关的挑战，例如考虑混杂变量（或元数据）的影响。混杂变量影响输入训练数据和目标输出之间的关系。当我们根据此类数据训练模型时，混杂变量会使学习特征的分布产生偏差。最近有前途的解决方案是元数据标准化（MDN），它基于不可训练的封闭式解决方案来估计元数据和每个特征之间的线性关系。然而，这种估计受到小批量样本大小的限制，因此可能导致该方法在训练期间不稳定。在本文中，我们通过应用惩罚方法（简称PDMN）来扩展MDN方法。我们将问题转化为双层嵌套优化问题。然后，我们使用惩罚方法来近似这个优化问题，以便 MDN 层内的线性参数可以在所有样本上进行训练和学习。这使得 PMDN 能够插入任何架构，甚至是那些不适合运行批处理级操作的架构，例如变压器和循环模型。我们在合成实验和多标签、多站点磁共振图像 (MRI) 数据集中使用 PMDN 而非 MDN，展示了模型准确性的提高以及对混杂因素的更大独立性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于惩罚方法的改进型元数据归一化（PDMN）方法，解决原有MDN方法在处理混杂变量时的稳定性问题，并提升模型性能。
AI.motivation: 现有MDN方法因受限于小批量样本量导致训练不稳定，且难以适配如Transformer等复杂模型架构，需更通用的解决方案。
AI.method: 将MDN问题转化为双层嵌套优化问题，通过惩罚方法使MDN层参数可训练，实现跨全部样本的参数学习，兼容多种模型架构。
AI.result: 在合成实验和多中心MRI数据集上，PDMN相较MDN显著提升模型准确性，并降低对混杂变量的依赖。
AI.conclusion: PDMN通过参数可训练性改进和架构兼容性设计，有效解决了混杂变量问题，为临床机器学习模型的可解释性提供了新方法。
